name: Adeena
characteristics: Adeena has shown herbal and spicy flavor characteristics, with light floral, lemon and pine notes. It lends a low, smooth bitterness with a clean finish
heritage: Adeena is a cross between Summit and an English male.

name: Admiral
characteristics: Admiral hops have aroma descriptors that include pleasant, resinous hop aroma with hints of citrus (orange) and herbal flavors.  Its mild aroma characteristics make it suitable for late-hopping and dry hopping applications in combination with other varieties.
heritage: The Admiral hop is a cross between Challenger and Northdown. It was bred by Wye College and released in 1998.

name: African Queen
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, Belgian Ale, Saison
characteristics: The aroma profile of the African Queen hop includes dank, blueberries, stone fruit, black currant, gooseberries, bubble gum, lemongrass, and chilies. This hop also works well with subtle blended beers such as those with coffee, fruit, and spices.
heritage: African Queen is a diploid seedling selected from a cross between 91J7/25 and an SA male 94US2/118. It was released in 2014.
readmore: Southern Hemisphere Hops: Superlatives Edition|https://beermaverick.com/southern-hemisphere-hops-superlatives-edition/

name: Agnus
characteristics: Agnus hops have key flavors that include lavender, leather and tobacco.
heritage: Agnus hops were bred from a number of varieties including Bor, Fuggles, Northern Brewer, Saaz and Sladek. Released in 2001 from the Czech Republic.

name: Ahhhroma
characteristics: With high oil content and unusually high percentages of myrcene, Ahhhroma is an ideal hop for dry hopping. Together, its complex tropical fruit aromas are often described as “pina colada.”

name: Ahtanum
characteristics: Ahtanum hops have aroma descriptors of citrus grapefruit and geranium and along with floral, piney and earth tones. Not as bitter as other hops, and used primarily for its aromatic properties.
heritage: Ahtanum was bred by Yakima Chief Ranches. Lineage is unknown except for the fact that it was open pollinated. It was released by YCR in 1997.
statistics:
Ahtanum has the second highest increase of production yield between 2014-2019 of existing hops that were not newly introduced.|
In 2019, Ahtanum was the 26th most produced hop in the United States.

name: Akoya
beer_styles: Alt, Pilsner, Lager, Helles, Kolsch, Golden Ale, Bitter, English IPA
hop_sub: Perle (US), Perle (GR), Aurora, Hallertau Tradition, Northern Brewer (US), Northern Brewer
characteristics: A classic aroma hop and was crossed in the Hopsteiner breeding program from the English variety Zenith and a male Hopsteiner breeding line. The aroma of raw hops can be described as herbal, citrus and tea-like, as well as slightly fruity with mint notes. Good resistance to diseases such as downy mildew and powdery mildew and Vertilicium wilt as well as drought stress. Main growing country is Germany.
heritage: Akoya was bred as a cross between Zenith and a Hopsteiner male.

name: Aloha Blend
characteristics: This unique BarthHaas Hop blend gives your beer an exotic boost with a flavor coming directly from Hawaii.

name: Altus
characteristics: Altus has massive resinous, as well as spicy and tangerine aromas.
heritage: 50 % Apollo, 25 % Wye Target

name: Amarillo
beer_styles: Pale Ale, IPA, Porter, Wheat Beer, Amber Ale
characteristics:
Amarillo hops impart a distinct flowery, spicy, tropical, citrus-like flavor and aroma in beer. The citrus has qualities of orange and lemon, like Cascade, but much stronger. Other aroma descriptors include grapefruit, melon, apricot and peach.

Amarillo hops offer solid bittering properties in the 9-11% Alpha Acid range. It also packs one of the highest myrcene oil contents (60-70% of total oils) of any hop. This extremely high Myrcene content gives it a very sweet citrus flavor and aroma that is closer to oranges than grapefruit.

Amarillo has become one of craft brewer's most favorite hops. It is often paired with Citra, Simcoe, and Mosaic in many juicy IPAs and pale ales.
heritage:
The Amarillo hop variety was accidentally discovered by Virgil Gamache Farms in 1990 when they found it growing alongside their Liberty hop field. They began cultivating it in 1991, then patented it as a new variety in 2003. It was originally patented under the identifier of VGXP01. It was released to the public shortly after receiving the patent.

Amarillo is sometimes referred to as Amarillo Gold.

statistics:
Amarillo had the 2nd most harvest in the US of the new varieties introduced between 2014 and 2019.|
In the crop year 2019, Amarillo hops were the 9th most harvested hop in the United States.

name: Amethyst
characteristics: The Amethyst hops have aroma characters that include subtl citrus, earthy and spicy notes.
heritage: Amethyst is a derivative of Saaz.

name: Apollo
hop_sub: Magnum (US), Magnum (GR), Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Nugget
characteristics: Apollo is a super high-alpha variety with a low cohumulone level that makes it an excellent bittering hop. When Apollo is used toward the end of the boil it can contribute flavors and aromas of citrus (lime), grapefruit, orange, pine, resin, and cannabis.
heritage: Apollo is a cross between Zeus and a male with (98001 x USDA 19058m) lineage. It was released in 2006.
statistics: Apollo was the 11th most produced hop in the United States in 2019

name: Apolon
characteristics: Traditionally used solely for bittering, Apolon hops can be used as both an aroma and a bittering hop and is considered excellent for both.
heritage: Originally introduced as a Super Styrian in the 1970’s, it has since been reclassified as a Slovenian hybrid and is a cross between Brewer’s Gold and a Yugoslavian wild male. It was a seedling selection No. 18/57 made in the early 1970's at the Hop Research Institute, Zalec, Slovenia, Yugoslavia, by Dr. Tone Wagner.

name: Aquila
characteristics:
No longer in production. 

Aquila hops had limited use due to high cohumolone levels.
heritage: Aquila was released in 1994 in the Pacific Northwest.

name: Aramis
beer_styles: Saison, Belgian Ale, French Ale, Trappist, Porter, Pale Ale, Weizenbier, Pilsner, Lager
hop_sub: Willamette, Challenger, Ahtanum, Centennial, Strisselspalt, EKG, Saaz (US), Hallertau Mittelfruh
characteristics: Aramis is an aroma variety with sweet and spicy characteristics. It resembles Strisselspalt, but contains higher oil and alpha content. Specific aroma descriptors include spicy, herbal and subtle citrus.
heritage: Aramis is the product of a 2002 cross between the French variety Strisselspalt and the English variety WGV (Whitbread Golding Variety).

name: Arcadian
characteristics: Noted aromas of the Arcadian hop includes light floral and orange and/or tangerine.
heritage: Created as a "field grade" hop by Great Lake Hops.

name: Archer
characteristics: Archer hops are said to have delicate and soft apricot, floral, lime and peach aromas. It combines classic assertive British aroma with a citrus twist. This hop can be useful when used as a dry hop addition.
heritage: Archer is a hop derived from a seedling of Sovereign and planted alongside Minstrel. Released by Charles Faram in 2013.

name: Ariana
hop_sub: Huell Melon, Mandarina Bavaria, Hallertau Blanc
characteristics: Ariana hops have key flavors that include passion fruit, pineapple, jasmine and tangerine. It is also fruity, with intense berry character of black currant and gooseberry. Also has citrus high notes (particularly grapefruit) with slight resinous and herbal tones
heritage: Ariana was bred by the  Hüll Hop Research Center, and a cross between Herkules and a wild male hop.

name: Astra
beer_styles: Ale, Pale Ale, IPA, Lager, Saison
characteristics: Astra hops have sweet tropical fruit flavors and New World characteristics. Flavor profiles with green melon, white peaches, white wine and a light grassiness, grapefruit and honey, the hop balances out nicely between a sweetness with a bit of a bite.
heritage: After several years and breeding by Ellerslie Hop Australia in Victoria, Australia, Astra was first released commercially in 2016.

name: Atlas
characteristics: Atlas has intense aroma notes of lime, floral (blossom) and pine.
heritage: Atlas is the daughter of Brewer's Gold. It was released in the 1970s by the Hop Research Institute in Zalec by Dr. Tone Wagner. Also known as Styrian Atlas.

name: Aurora
beer_styles: Ale, Lager, Belgian Ale, English Ale
hop_sub: Styrian Golding, Perle (GR), Perle (US), Northern Brewer (US), Northern Brewer (GR)
characteristics: Aurora hops have aroma descriptors that include floral, spice and lemongrass. It displays intense yet pleasant aroma in finished beers.
heritage: Aurora is a cross between Northern Brewer and a TG seedling originating in Yugoslavia. It is also known as Super Styrian or Styrian Aurora.

name: Aurum
characteristics: Aurum hops combine hoppy nuances with a fruity taste. They are suitable for classic German beers with spicy, fruity and floral aromas. It has exceptionally fine and delicate classic hop aromas with spicy-herbal notes and - depending on the timing of its addition - fresh citrus aromas.
heritage: Sometimes referred to as Tettnanger Aurum. This hop is bred from a mother Tettnanger landrace and a father with the Hull breeding line of 91/36/04.

name: Azacca
beer_styles: Pale Ale, IPA, Fruit Beer, Sour, Saison
characteristics: Azacca has an amazingly refreshing aroma. It's descriptions usually include aromas and flavors of mango, papaya, orange, grapefruit, lemon, pine, spice, pineapple, grassy, tropical fruit, and citrus. However, the Azacca hop has a very delicate hop aroma that can be easily overpowered by other hops and flavors.
heritage: Azacca is a descendant of Northern Brewer and Summit it's a cross between Toyomidori and an unknown variety. Bred by the American Dwarf Hop Association, it was released in 2013.
readmore: The Most Common Hops Used in IPAs|https://beermaverick.com/the-most-common-hops-used-in-ipas/
statistics:
Azacca production grew by 339% between 2014 and 2019.|
In 2019, Azacca was the 18th most grown hop in the United States.

name: Banner
characteristics:
Banner is no longer in production.

It had high alpha-acids and a pleasant aroma, but due to poor stability and disease susceptibility, it was never commercially viable.
heritage: Banner hops were bred from a Brewers Gold seedling in the early 1970s through open pollination. It was released in 1996 after Anheuser Busch decided to use it.

name: Barbe Rouge
beer_styles: Belgian Ale, Amber, Pilsner, Bière de Garde, Pale Ale, Black IPA, Porter, Saison
characteristics: The Barbe Rouge hop is an aroma variety that features notes of citrus fruit and fine red berries. Kumquat, orange, lime, redcurrant, strawberry, and raspberry are all represented in Barbe Rouge’s delicate aromatic attributes.
heritage: The Barbe Rouge hop was developed as part of the varietal research program in Alsace, France. It has parentage in Strisselspalt.

name: Beata
characteristics: The high beta acid of the Beata hop means that its bitterness utilization is very good. It also has notes of almonds, honey and apricots.
heritage: Bred for its beta content at Horticulture Research International (HRI) at Wye College in the UK in 1995, it went to farm trials in 2006.

name: Belma
beer_styles: Pale Ale, IPA, Blonde Ale, Pilsner, Wheat, Hazy IPA, Porter
characteristics: Belma has been tested as a dual-use hop and found to have an ambrosial mix of orange, melon, strawberry and pineapple with a slight hint of grapefruit
heritage: The Belma hop is a daughter of Magnum and Kitamidori. It was released in 2012.

name: Bergamot
characteristics: Bergamot subtly nicknamed "O.A.F." by the brewers in the GLH Brew Group that have crafted incredible first round trial brews with this hop.  There is no lack of orange in this hop.  You won't need to garnish your beer with an orange slice ever again.
readmore: Michigan Grown Hops: The Industry’s Best Kept Secret|https://beermaverick.com/michigan-grown-hops-tasting-trials-glh/

name: Bianca
characteristics: While not bred for commercial use, it is said that the Bianca cones can be used for flavoring if a Saazer-style aroma and taste is desired. Sunbeam, Bianca’s half-sister has similar characteristics.
heritage: Bianca was bred strictly as an ornamental hop.

name: Bitter Gold
beer_styles: Belgian Ale, Pale Ale, IPA, ESB, Pilsner
characteristics: Bitter Gold offers limited aroma when used as a bittering hop but delivers diverse stone and tropical fruit flavors in later additions.
heritage: Bitter Gold was released in 1999. Its lineage includes Brewer’s Gold, Bullion, Comet and Fuggle.

name: Boadicea
characteristics: Boadicea hops have aroma descriptors that include floral, orchard blossom and ripe fruit.
heritage: Boadicea is a dwarf variety derived from open pollination of a second-generation wild, Japanese female hop.

name: Bobek
beer_styles: Ale, Pilsner, Lager, Strong Bitter, ESB
characteristics: Bobek has aroma descriptors that include intense and pleasant with floral and pine overtones.
heritage: Bobek is a diploid hybrid of Northern Brewer and a Tettnanger seedling of unknown origin.

name: Bohemie
characteristics: Bohemie hops are suitable for second hop addition.
heritage: Bohemie breeding is based on varieties of Saaz and Sladek.

name: Boomerang
characteristics: The Boomerang hop aroma is intensively spicy and citrusy. Boomerang is suitable for all hop additions.
heritage: Boomerang was released in 2017 and originated from the Angus hop.

name: Bor
characteristics: Bor, which means pine, is a dual purpose variety named after the pinewoods which are typical for a region in the Czech Republic.
heritage: It was derived from Northern Brewer, registered in 1994

name: Bouclier
characteristics: Bouclier’ s combination of French and English aromas allows it to bring a French touch to English-style beers. It has aroma descriptors that include herbal, grass and spicy. Sometime referred to as "Shield".
heritage: Developed in 2005 as a cross between Strisselspalt and a UK male, Bouclier is the most recent release from the French varietal research program. Its UK lineage includes Wye Challenger, Early Bird Golding and Northern Brewer.

name: Bramling Cross
beer_styles: ESB, Bitter, Pale Ale, Spiced Ale, Scotch Ale
hop_sub: East Kent Goldings, Bullion, Northern Brewer (US), Northern Brewer (GR), Galena
characteristics: Bramling Cross hops have aroma descriptors that include strong spice, blackcurrant, loganberry and lemon.  It is often used in traditional cask conditioned beers due to its distinct characteristics.
heritage: Bramling Cross is a cross between Bramling (a traditional Golding variety) and a wild Manitoban (Canadian) hop. It was bred by Wye College in 1927.

name: Bravo
beer_styles: American IPA, American Pale Ale, American Stout
hop_sub: Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Zeus, Chinook, Centennial, Nugget
characteristics: The Bravo hops have aroma descriptors include orange, vanilla and floral.  It is an excellent bittering variety and can provide pleasant fruit and sweet floral aroma characteristics in some applications.
heritage: Bravo's lineage includes Zeus crossed with a male (98004 x USDA 19058m). It was released in 2006.
statistics: In 2019, Bravo was the 25th most produced hop in the US.|Between 2014 and 2019, Bravo's harvested pounds decreased by 63%.

name: Brewer's Gold (GR)
beer_styles: Stout, Dark Ale, Belgian Ale, English Ale, German Ale
characteristics: Brewer's Gold hops have aroma descriptors that include blackcurrant, fruity, and spicy.
heritage: Brewer's Gold was bred in 1917 and first produced in 1919, Brewer’s Gold is one of the first varieties to emerge from a UK breeding program by professor E.S. Salmon. It is an open pollinated cross of a Manitoban wild hop (BB1) originating in England.

name: Brewer's Gold (US)
beer_styles: German Ale, Belgian Ale, English Ale
hop_sub: Galena, Brewer's Gold, Chinook, Galena, Nugget
characteristics: It is an English variety, however American-grown Brewer’s Gold contains higher levels of alpha acids than its English counterpart. This hop has hints of black currant and spicy aromas.
heritage: Bred at Wye College in 1919, Brewer’s Gold is an ancestor to many major high alpha hops including Sterling, Galena, Horizon, Centennial and Nugget.

name: Brooklyn
characteristics:
Brooklyn was renamed Moutere (due to a certain brewery in NYC taking notice).

Brooklyn is a big hop with high alpha acid content, however, sensory panels have also indicated the presence of grapefruit, tropical fruit and passion fruit characteristics. Delivers intense fruity oils with top notes of baking spice and sweet hay.
heritage: Released in 2015, Brooklyn is a triploid variety from New Zealand Southern Cross and a selected New Zealand male.

name: BRU-1
beer_styles: Wheats, Pale Ale, IPA, New England IPA
characteristics: BRU-1 hops offer true pineapple flavor. BRU-1 is notable for its distinct sweet fruit aroma that is often described as pineapple. When used as a whirlpool or dry hop addition, BRU-1 delivers the aroma of freshly cut pineapple and green fruits. BRU-1 is synergistic with other hops creating a depth of fruit flavor. BRU-1 has also been shown to improve haze stability in certain beer styles.
heritage: BRU-1 was developed through open pollination at Brulotte Farms in Toppenish, WA.

name: Bullion
hop_sub: Brewers Gold, Northern Brewer (US), Northern Brewer (GR), Galena, Bramling Cross, Mount Ranier
characteristics: Bullion hops have strong and zesty blackcurrant characteristics.
heritage:
Bullion was pollinated from a seedling of English Brewer's Gold a Wild
Manitoba hop (BB1).  It was a major variety throughout the mid-1940s,
however commercial production ceased in 1985 due to newer varieties with higher alpha acid content and better storage stability.

name: Buzz Bullets
characteristics: Buzz Bullets is a hop blend with citrus and floral notes. It is also known to have clean bitterness.
heritage: Buzz Bullets is a proprietary blend from Yakima Valley Hops.

name: Caliente
beer_styles: IPA, Wheat, Pale Ale, Spice Beer
characteristics: Caliente hops have flavors of citrus (lemon zest), peach and pine, with aromas of stone fruit and mandarin. While "caliente" means "hot" in Spanish, this hop is not spicy.

name: Callista
characteristics: Key flavors of the Callista hop include strawberry, pear, caramel, passion fruit and orange. Callista kicks off intense fruit flavors of passion fruit, apricot, peach, and blackberry, plus some pine. Low alpha acids ensure this will be a late-addition hop.
heritage: Callista's parentage include Hallertau Tradition crossed with a Huell male.  It is also known as Grungeist.

name: Calypso
beer_styles: Pale Ale, IPA, Barleywine, Golden Ales, Stout, Porter, Bitter
characteristics: The Calypso hop has a crisp and fruity aroma. Flavors of apples, pears, stone fruit and lime citrus also are noted.
heritage: Calypso is a diploid aroma-type hop, originated from a cross between Hopsteiner breeding female 98005 and a Hopsteiner male derived from Nugget and USDA 19058m.

name: Canadian Redvine
characteristics: Canadian Redvine has such a high cohumulone content and low alphas, it is not commonly used. It is said to impart mild flavors of cherry, berries, pine and even citrus like grapefruit. Some brewers have reported a catty character.
heritage: Not used much anymore. Submitted to the USDA in 1993.

name: Cascade
beer_styles: American Ale, IPA, Porter, Barleywine, Witbier, Pale Ale, American Pale Ales
characteristics:
Cascade is the hop variety that made hops famous. It single-handedly defined the American Pale Ale style. 

Defined by its citrus, and often more specifically grapefruit flavor, this hop accounts for around 10% of the US's harvest of hops. Cascade also has a medium-intense floral and spice citrus qualities. When used as a bittering hop, it imparts moderate bitterness. It is most famous for being the finishing hop in Sierra Nevada's Pale Ale, widely considered the beer that launched the IPA and bitter beer craze.

Cascade hops are widely available and are grown in multiple countries. It is usually packaged hop pellets, but is sometimes available fresh around harvest time. Cascade hops can grown at home since they are not a trademarked variety.
heritage:
Cascade was developed in the USDA breeding program at Oregon State University, and released to the public in 1972. It was originally created from a cross between Fuggle and the Russian hop Serebrianka in 1967. Cascade was initially only known by its number designation of USDA 56013.

Despite being known primarily as an American variety, today there are also New Zealand, Argentinian, and Australian varieties of Cascade.

name: Cashmere
characteristics: Cashmere has a complex and intensely fruity aroma, with strong overtones of lemon, lime, peach, and melon. Secondary notes of coconut, lemongrass, candy, and herbs can show through in whirlpool or dry hop additions
heritage: Cashmere is a cross between Cascade and Northern Brewer. It was released in 2013 by Washington State University.
statistics: In 2019, Cashmere was the 27th most produced hop in the United States

name: Cekin
characteristics: Cekin, along with its sibling, Cicero, is not grown in large quantities. Cekin is said to have a pleasing, distinctive and continental aroma comparable to Styrian Golding. Very little Cekin is grown commercially due to lack of acceptance by breweries.
heritage: Cekin's pedigree is aurora crossed with a tetraploid Yugoslav male 3/3.

name: Celeia
beer_styles: English Ale, Lager, Pilsner, English Ale, ESB, Red Ale
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors of this hop include noble characteristics. It is distinctively more floral than Styrian Golding or Fuggle hops.
heritage: Celeia is the triploid offspring of Styrian Golding, Aurora and a Slovenian wild hop.  Also referred to as Styrian Golding Celeia.

name: Centennial
hop_sub: Cascade, Chinook, Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Amarillo
characteristics: Centennial hops are very similar to Cascade and are characterized by aromatic pine, citrus, and floral notes. It is characterized as having rounded and medium intense floral, citrus and grapefruit flavors and aromas. Other flavors of the Centennial hop include pine needles and tangerines.
heritage: Centennial hops were first bred in 1974 with a cross between a Brewers Gold and Fuggle hops. Other sources mention it was a cross between Brewers Gold, Fuggle, East Kent Golding, and Bavarian hops. It was named after the Washington State Centennial Celebration, which occurred in 1989, just before it was released to the public in 1990.

statistics: In 2019, Centennial was the 6th most harvested hop in the US.|Production of the Centennial hop increased by 26% between 2014 and 2019.

name: Challenger
hop_sub: Northern Brewer (US), Northern Brewer (GR), Perle (US), Target, Northdown
characteristics:
Challenger hops have aroma descriptors that include cedar, green tea and sweet fruit.  It is a versatile variety with wide application in both
early and late kettle additions.
heritage: Bred at Wye College and released for commercial planting in 1972, the Challenger hop is the granddaughter of Northern Brewer crossed with a downy mildew resistant male and is a 'cousin' of Target. It is a niece of Northdown.

name: Chelan
characteristics: Chelan is a bittering hop that has mild fruity, floral and citrus aromas.
heritage: Developed by the John I Haas, Inc. breeding company, released in 1994. It is a daughter of Galena.

name: Chinook
beer_styles: American Pale Ale, IPA, Stout, Porter, Lager, Winter Ale
hop_sub: Nugget, Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Northern Brewer (US), Galena
characteristics: Chinook can be slightly spicy and have a smoky earthiness quality. It has an impressive pine and resin character, with distinct spice and grapefruit. Use sparingly in the boil as it can add a harsh bitterness if overused.
heritage: The Chinook hop is a cross between a Petham Golding and a USDA-selected male with high alpha-acids and good storage properties.
readmore: The Most Common Hops Used in IPAs|https://beermaverick.com/the-most-common-hops-used-in-ipas/
statistics: In 2019, Chinook was the 8th most harvested hop in the US.|Between 2014 and 2019, Chinook's production (in pounds) grew by 46%.

name: Citra
beer_styles: American Pale Ale, IPA, Double IPA, Pale Ales
characteristics:
The Citra hop is a high alpha acid hop with a strong, yet smooth floral and citrus aroma and flavor. It has specific aroma descriptors that include grapefruit, citrus, peach, melon, lime, gooseberry, passion fruit and lychee. These tropical fruit flavors have made Citra the most popular hop to craft brewers in the world. 

Citra adds a smooth bitterness when added to the boil. This is due to Citra having a fairly high alpha acids percentage, while also having enough flavor and aroma to be considered an aroma hop. 

Citra overtook Cascade as the most grown hop variety in 2018 and has yet to relinquish that title. Citra has redefined what could be expected out of a hop. It helped push IPAs to the forefront of craft brewing popularity with its extreme citrus flavors.
heritage:
Citra was developed by the Hop Breeding Company of Yakima, WA and released to the public in 2008. 

Gene Probasco, who led the hop breeding program at John I. Haas for 39 years, originally created the plant that would eventually become Citra back in 1990. Gene crossed two unnamed varieties and formed what was known as X-114 or HBC 394 for years. While the two hop varieties directly used to create it were unknown, their parentage included American Tettnanger, Brewer’s Gold, Hallertau Mittelfrüh, East Kent Goldings and an unknown hop variety, most likely an American wild hop.

A single plant was selected by hop farmer Jason Perrault in 1992 and then expanded to a test plot of four plants in 1993. This plot was located in Washington state. The evaluation went so well that by 2003 it was expanded to 21 plants. It would be more than four additional years before the hop would be ready for commercial tests.

name: Cluster
characteristics: Cluster has specific aroma descriptors that include floral, earthy and sweet fruit. Cluster is an excellent dual-purpose hop and is often used in the reproduction of historical beer styles.
heritage: Cluster's pedigree is not known but it is possibly the result of a cross between an English variety and an American male hop. Cluster is one of the oldest hop varieties grown in the United States.

name: Cluster Fugget Blend
characteristics: Cluster Fugget™ is a spontaneous hop blend that is just that...a cluster Fugget of Pacific Northwestern varieties, capturing the adventurous spirit of the home brewing community. Whether it is your first batch, or your one-hundredth, every home brewer has wound up brewing a beer with a cluster Fugget of aromas and flavors. This hop blend was created in celebration of these fortunate mistakes, reminding us that no brew should be taken too seriously.

name: Columbia
characteristics: The aroma of the Columbia hop is similar to that of Chinook, but not as strong. It includes crisp pineapple, bright and pungent lemon-citrus notes.
heritage: The Columbia hop was developed in the 1960s in Corvallis, Oregon. Its lineage includes a tetraploid Fuggle (USDA 21003) and a Fuggle seedling 2-4. Columbia is also a sister of Willamette. Budweiser originally helped develop this hop, but instead decided to go with Willamette, leaving this available to other brewers. Columbia was returned to production in 2011 after being stopped in the 1980s.

name: Columbus
beer_styles: IPA, American Pale Ale, Stout, Barleywine, Lager
hop_sub: CTZ, Tomahawk, Zeus, Centennial, Chinook, Galena, Nugget, Millennium
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors of the Columbus hop includes earthiness, black pepper, licorice, spice (curry) and subtle citrus. The floral and citrus notes from the Columbus hop come out in both aroma and flavor, but can be pungent. This strong flavor and aroma make the Columbus hop great for late additions to a boil or dry-hopping.
heritage:
Columbus has an unknown origin. It is often referred to as CTZ (Columbus / Tomahawk / Zeus) due to Hopunion and YCH attempting to register the same hop with different names. After an agreement was reached between the two names, both names were registered. They are technically the same hop however. It is genetically distinct from Zeus hops, but has a very similar profile. 

The exact lineage of Columbus is unknown, however it is widely assumed that Brewer’s Gold and several undisclosed American varieties played significant parenting roles. It was developed in the 1980s by Charles Zimmerman who had worked for the U.S. Department of Agriculture until 1979 and who subsequently held positions with various private hop-processing and trading companies.

statistics: In 2019, the CTZ mix of hops was the number one hop in terms of production yield in the United States.|Over 17 million pounds of CTZ hops were harvested in 2019.|CTZ has been the most produced hop in the US for the last 5 years.

name: Comet
characteristics: Comet hops have aroma descriptors that include subtle, wild American grassy and grapefruit.
heritage: Comet was originally released as a high alpha by the USDA in 1974. Its parentage is English Sunshine and a native American hop giving it a wild American characteristic. Commercial production ceased in the 1980s in favor of newer super-alpha hops.

name: Contessa
characteristics: This noble hop variety elicits aromas of green tea, floral, lemongrass and light pear. According to Hopsteiner, Contessa's aroma lends a smooth and delicate bitterness while delivering an elegant fragrance, making it a perfect fit for the lager beer style and unique addition to many others.
heritage: A cross out of Fuggle and a Cascade male. Also called experimental #04190

name: Crystal
beer_styles: Lager, Kolsch, ESB, Pilsner, IPA, Pale Ale, Belgian Ale
hop_sub: Mount Hood, Hersbrucker, Strisselspalt, Liberty, Ultra, Hallertau
characteristics: The Crystal hop has aromas of woods, green, floral and fruity with herb and spice notes of cinnamon, nutmeg and black pepper.
heritage: Crystal is a triploid of Hallertau Mittelfrueh crossed with (Cascade x USDA 65009-64034M). It is also the half-sister of Mt. Hood, Liberty and Ultra.
statistics: In 2019, Crystal was the 22nd most produced hop in the United States.|Production of the Crystal hop has decreased by 5% between 2014 and 2019.

name: CTZ
beer_styles: IPA, American Pale Ale, Stout, Barleywine, Lager, Imperial Stout
characteristics: CTZ hops have aroma descriptors that include black pepper, licorice, curry and subtle citrus. CTZ stands for (Columbus Tomahawk and Zeus), which are three slightly different varieties. All have virtually identical brewing values and aromas. It can be used in the boil, whirlpool or via dry hopping.
heritage: Although genetically different, Zeus, Columbus and Tomahawk are often referred to as part of CTZ. CTZ however is not a specific hop, but instead a name given to a trio of similar hops. The exact lineage of CTZ is unknown, however it is widely assumed that Brewer’s Gold and several undisclosed American varieties played significant parenting roles. It was developed in the 1980s by Charles Zimmerman who had worked for the U.S. Department of Agriculture until 1979 and who subsequently held positions with various private hop-processing and trading companies. CTZ is one of the most produced hops every crop year.
readmore: The Rise and Eventual Fall of the CTZ Hop|https://beermaverick.com/the-rise-and-eventual-fall-of-the-ctz-hop/

name: Dana
characteristics: The Dana hop displays subtle floral, lemon and pine aroma characteristics.
heritage: Dana is a cross between German Magnum and a wild Slovenian hop.

name: Defender
characteristics: Defender hops add a pleasant, European-style aroma.
heritage: Defender was bred from a New Mexico Wild American female, Eastwell Golding and other English hops, it was selected in the early 1960’s at Wye College in England.

name: Delta
characteristics: Delta hops have a mild and pleasant aroma that is slightly spicy with a hint of citrus and melon.
heritage: Delta is a cross between Fuggle and a male derived from Cascade. It was released in 2009.

name: Diamant
characteristics: Diamant is a further extension of the Saaz range with improved plant health and higher agronomic performance compared to the mother "Spalter". With its classic fresh, hoppy aroma profile, Diamant is particularly suitable for harmonious lager beers with a fine hop aroma.
heritage: Diamant is a daughter of Spalter and a male derived at the Hüll breeding institute. It was released in 2019.

name: Diamond Springs
characteristics: Diamond Springs hops have an interesting aroma according to their breeder GLH. Aromas include bright citrus, lemon and mint.
heritage: Diamond Springs is a "field grade" hop created by Great Lake Hops (GLH).

name: Dr. Rudi
characteristics: Dr. Rudi hops have specific aroma descriptors that include resin, pine, and lemongrass.
heritage: Dr. Rudi is a triploid bred from an open cross of Smoothcone. It was released in 1976. However, its name was officially changed to Dr. Rudi in 2012.

name: East Kent Goldings
characteristics: East Kent Golding hops have aroma descriptors that include smooth and delicate with floral, lavender, spice, honey, earth, lemon and thyme overtones.
heritage: EKG's lineage is unknown other than it was used in England going all the way back to 1790.

name: Eclipse
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, NEIPA, Lager, Wheat Ale
characteristics: When Eclipse is used in the whirlpool and dry hop, it brings an unmistakably sweet mandarin flavor to beer with strong notes of citrus peel and fresh pine needles.
heritage: Eclipse’s ancestry can be traced as far back as 1915, although it was first developed by HPA in 2004 and released commercially in 2020. The subsequent generations of cross pollination featured varieties such as Fuggle, Brewer’s Gold, Comet and Pride of Ringwood in the uniquely Australian environment.

name: Ekuanot
beer_styles: American Pale Ale, American IPA, American Wheat, Saison, Sour, Specialty IPA, Pilsner
characteristics: Ekuanot has fruity aromatics such as lemon, lime, pithy orange, tropical fruit, berry, papaya, and sometimes apple. Along with these there may be more herbal notes, such as sage and eucalyptus.
heritage: The Ekuanot hop was developed by the Hop Breeding Company and first released in 2014 as HBC 366
readmore: Why the Equinox, Denali and Stella Hops Changed Their Names|https://beermaverick.com/why-equinox-denali-and-stella-hops-changed-their-names/
beer_examples: Lagunitas Equinox Pale Ale|https://www.lagunitas.com/beer/equinox|Great Divide Hop Disciples IPA - Ekuanot|https://www.beeradvocate.com/beer/profile/158/321794/
statistics:
Ekuanot production grew by the 8th most in the US from 2014-2019.|
In 2019, Ekuanot was the 16th most produced hop in the United States.

name: El Dorado
beer_styles: American Pale Ale, IPA, Lager, Wheats, Cream Ale
characteristics: El Dorado hops elicit responses of fruity notes and tropical fruit flavors. When used as a bittering hop, El Dorado lends a soft and balanced bitterness. When used in later additions El Dorado brings bright tropical fruit flavors and aromas of pear, watermelon, candy and stone fruit.
heritage: The lineage for the El Dorado hop goes back to Brewer's Gold, Bullion, Comet and Fuggle and was released in 2010 by CLS Farms in Moxee, Washington.
readmore: The Most Common Hops Used in IPAs|https://beermaverick.com/the-most-common-hops-used-in-ipas/

name: Elixir
characteristics: Elixir is a robust and complex hop that offers unique aromas of Cognac, leather, and tobacco. It has complementary aromas of sweet citrus and tropical fruits. Elixir is well suited for darker styles but can also be incorporated into fruit-forward styles or even spicy saisons.
heritage: Elixir was developed by the Comptoir Agricole breeding program in Alsace, France.

name: Ella
characteristics: Ella a pleasant floral aroma that is backed by soft spice and also has described to include distinct grapefruit and tropical flavors.  It is reminiscent of a noble variety in lower doses, but imparts strong tropical fruit flavors in larger additions.
heritage: Ella and Galaxy share the same mother, but Ella's father is a variety from Spalt, Germany. It was bred in 2001 and released to the commercially in 2007. Formerly known as Stella, but the name changed due to trademark issues with a certain brewery by the same name.
readmore: Southern Hemisphere Hops: Superlatives Edition|https://beermaverick.com/southern-hemisphere-hops-superlatives-edition/
statistics: Ella is Australia's third-most produced hop behind Galaxy and Vic Secret, as of 2019.|In 2019, Australia produced 133 metric tonnes of Ella, which was an increase of 6.4% over 2018.

name: Emerald Spire
characteristics: The Emerald Spire hop is pleasant, fruit forward and delicious.  If the smell of the cones doesn't make your mouth water the first sip of any beer with this hop as the star of the show sure will.
readmore: Michigan Grown Hops: The Industry’s Best Kept Secret|https://beermaverick.com/michigan-grown-hops-tasting-trials-glh/

name: Endeavour
characteristics: Complex blackcurrant, loganberry and spice notes best describe Endeavour's aroma, with a wonderful grapefruit and lime flavour. It is gentler than Cascade.
heritage: Endeavour is a cross between Cascade and a Hedgerow Hop. It is also the granddaughter of Target. It was bred in 2002 at Wye College, England.

name: Enigma
characteristics: The Enigma hop has distinct tropical fruit, berry, stone fruit aroma descriptors. It also has hints of juicy red fruits like raspberries and red currants, but what sets it apart from other varieties are the white wine notes.
heritage: Enigma is a hop variety released by the Hop Products Australia in 2013 with a breeding program using Swiss Tettnang and North American hops. It was originally cultivated in 2002.
readmore: Southern Hemisphere Hops: Superlatives Edition|https://beermaverick.com/southern-hemisphere-hops-superlatives-edition/
statistics: Enigma is Australia's fifth most produced hop as of 2019.|In 2019, Australia produced 90 metric tonnes of Ella, which was an increase of 125% over 2018.

name: Epic
characteristics: Early brewing with the Epic hop indicates medium intensity, deep fruit and berry-like aromas without any citrus or floral notes. Epic is an excellent late aroma hop. Well balanced, rounded, mild and fruity when used in bittering addition.
heritage: Found as a chance seedling in 1987 and grown as an ornamental garden plant until 2004, Epic was expanded for commercial production in winter 2014-2015. The history of the field, combined with the oil composition of Epic, strongly suggests that the lineage includes Alliance hops.

name: Equinox
characteristics: Notes of lemon and lime citrus, fruits like papaya and apple, green peppers and herbs
heritage:
Also known as HBC 366. Developed by The Hop Breeding Company in Washington state in 2014.

Renamed as Ekuanot due to trademark issues.
readmore: Why the Equinox, Denali and Stella Hops Changed Their Names|https://beermaverick.com/why-equinox-denali-and-stella-hops-changed-their-names/

name: Ernest
characteristics:
Aroma: Fruity - apricot, citrus and spice.
Bittering: It has clear 'New World' characteristics producing a complex flavour with predominantly fruity notes, especially apricot, citrus and spice.
heritage: Ernest is named after its breeder, Prof Ernest Salmon. It was selected at Wye College, Kent as a seedling code OZ97a in 1923 from seed collected in 1921 as an open-pollination of Neomex AA7.

name: Eroica
beer_styles: Pale Ale, Dark Ale, Stout, Amber Ale, Porter, ESB
hop_sub: Brewer's Gold, Chinook, Cluster, Galena, Nugget
characteristics: Flavor-wise, the Eroica hop features a sharp fruity essence. It is primarily used as a bittering hop.
heritage: Eroica was released in 1982 and is a descendant of Brewer's Gold and sister to Galena

name: Eureka
hop_sub: Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Apollo, Merkur, Simcoe, Summit
characteristics: The Eureka hop has bold aromas of herbal notes, mint, pine, and other dark fruits. Other flavors include citrus and peach, aromas of stone fruit, blackcurrant and mandarin. Hopsteiner's Eureka! hop has high notes of fruit and citrus, but it is most well known for its herbal flavors. Coming in with an average of 3.5mL hop oils per 100g of hops, Eureka is loaded with flavorful oils to bring out it's mint and dank aromas.
heritage: Eureka is the child of Apollo and Merkur. Formerly known as Experimental Variety 05256.
statistics: Eurkea! was the 7th fasted growing hop in the US in terms of production between 2014 and 2019.|In 2019, Eureka was the 15th most produced hop in the United States.

name: Evergreen Blend
characteristics:
Evergreen announces its presence with strong suggestions of fresh peach and apricot, bright citrus (lemon candy
orange zest, tangerine), and watermelon candy. Green notes of fresh hay and pine needles round out a classic American craft beer hop profi le. Secondary and tertiary notes include menthol, dank, geranium, herbs (basil, dill, thyme), cannabis, and green tea.
heritage: Evergreen was developed by BSG Hop Solutions and added to their portfolio in 2021.

name: Falconer's Flight
hop_sub: Citra, Simcoe, Amarillo, Sorachi Ace, Summit, Cascade
characteristics: Falconer’s Flight hop pellets are an exclusive proprietary hop blend created to honor and support the legacy of Northwest brewing legend, Glen Hay Falconer. Proceeds from each Falconer’s Flight purchase is contributed to the Glen Hay Falconer Foundation. These hop pellets are an excellent complement to many IPA and Pale Ale-oriented hop varieties. Specific aroma descriptors include distinct tropical, floral, lemon and grapefruit characteristics.
heritage: Falconers Flight is a proprietary blend of Pacific Northwest hops that was developed in 2010 by Hopunion, LLC.

name: Falconer's Flight 7CS
beer_styles: American Pale Ale, IPA, Double IPA
hop_sub: CTZ, Centennial, Citra, Columbus, Chinook, Cluster, Cascade, Crystal
characteristics: This hop blend includes the 7 American "C" hops: Cascade, Centennial, Chinook, Citra, Cluster, Columbus and Crystal in addition to other experimental varieties. Specific aroma descriptors include distinct tropical, floral, lemon, pine and grapefruit characteristics.
heritage: A proprietary hop blend created by Hopunion.

name: Fantasia Blend
characteristics: This unique BarthHaas Group Hop Composition provides a silky touch of cream caramel and stone fruits.
heritage: Fantasia is a proprietary blend created by the Barth-Haas group.

name: Feux-Coeur Francais
heritage: Feux-Coeur Francais hops were first harvested in 2010 with genetic roots in Burgundian France.

name: First Gold
beer_styles: English Ale, Porter, Fruit Beer, Saison, Blonde Ale, zips
hop_sub: Whitbread Golding Variety, East Kent Golding, Willamette
characteristics: First Gold hops have aroma descriptors that include tangerine, orange marmalade, apricot, magnolia, red berries, herbal, orange and herbal. First Gold - also known as Prima Donna - has excellent aroma qualities and much of the flavor characteristics of WGV and has proven a success in stronger flavored summer beers and IPAs. This variety is suitable as a general kettle hop and also for late and dry hopping in all types of beer.
heritage: First Gold was bred at Wye College in 1995 from a cross pollination of WGV (Whitbread Golding Variety) with a dwarf male. This hop is also known as Prima Donna.

name: Flyer
characteristics: A citrus hop with aromas of stoned fruits, licorice, treacle-toffee and caramel with slight burnt notes. Its bittering characteristics can best be described as spicy, citrus, liquorice and resinous.
heritage: Flyer results from a cross made in 2002 between a high alpha-acid female breeding line and a low trellis-type male hop. Following promising results from advanced trial plots during 2007 and 2008, it was established on licensed farm trials with Wye Hops during 2009. The produce from the advanced plots was used for successful pilot brewing trials in 2010.

name: Fuggle
beer_styles: English Ale, American Pale Ale, Lambics, Brown Ale, Stout
hop_sub: Willamette, Styrian Golding, East Kent Goldings
characteristics: Fuggle hops have aroma descriptors that include mild, wood, grass, and mint.
heritage: Fuggle was selected as a chance seedling back in 1861. It was propagated by Richard Fuggle in Kent, England in 1875.

name: Gaia
characteristics: Gaia is characteristic for its hoppy and spicy aroma. Gaia is suitable not only for the first but for the second hopping as well.
heritage: Gaia was released in 2017 and originated from the Angus hop.

name: Galaxy
beer_styles: Pale Ale, IPA, Barley Wine, Fruit Beers, Saison, Wheat Beer, Wild Ale
characteristics:
Galaxy hops have one of the highest concentrations of essential oils known in hops, which has contributed to its meteoric rise in popularity. Galaxy is often described as having a strong passion fruit aroma and flavor as well as blasts of clean citrus and peach. At times, you can also experience pineapple and tropical fruit hints. Galaxy hops are all tropical fruit with little to no floral, pine, or spice flavors or aromas.

Galaxy is an extremely versatile hop variety. They can work compliment other hops perfectly, or take center stage as the primary flavor. Their high alpha acid content make them especially suited to late boil and dry hopping. Their distinctive mixture of citrus, passion fruit, peach and hints of grass is sure to stand out and create a beer like no other.
heritage: Australian Galaxy hops are Australia’s and Hop Products of Australia’s (HPA) greatest success story. Breeding started in 1994 when an Australian female plant known as J78, which is progeny of the variety Pride of Ringwood, was crossed with a male derived from the high alpha German Perle. Galaxy hops were developed by Hop Products Australia in the mid-1990s, but not released until 2009.
readmore: The Most Common Hops Used in IPAs|https://beermaverick.com/the-most-common-hops-used-in-ipas/
beer_examples: Hill Farmstead Double Galaxy IPA|https://hillfarmstead.com/beer/double-galaxy/;Toppling Goliath Twisted Galaxy|https://www.tgbrews.com/twisted-galaxy;Masthead Galaxy Vice|https://untappd.com/b/masthead-brewing-company-galaxy-vice/2846577
statistics: Galaxy is the most produced hop in Australia, accounting for over over 55% of the total hop production in the country as of 2019.|Galaxy hop production increased 8.6% in Australia from 2018 until 2019 to 822 metric tons of hops.|A $35 million project started in 2018 is aimed at increasing Galaxy hop production to over 1,281 metric tons by 2022.

name: Galena
beer_styles: American Ale, Stout, Lager, English Ale
hop_sub: Brewer's Gold, Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Nugget
characteristics: Aroma descriptors of the Galena hop include sweet fruits, pear, pineapple, blackcurrant, grapefruit, lime, gooseberry and spicy wood.
heritage: The Galena hop was an open pollinated seedling of Brewer's Gold. It was created in Idaho in 1968 and released in 1978.

name: Gemini
characteristics: Gemini is quickly becoming known as this year's ultimate utility power house.  This hop is no "one trick pony", offering endless opportunities to enhance and expand the flavor profile of a brew.
readmore: Michigan Grown Hops: The Industry’s Best Kept Secret|https://beermaverick.com/michigan-grown-hops-tasting-trials-glh/

name: Glacier
beer_styles: Pale Ale, ESB, English Pale Ale, Porter, Stout
characteristics: Glacier hops have aroma descriptors that include plum, blackberry and wood.
heritage: Glacier is a cross between Elsasser F and 8685-014 M. Genetic composition is 1/2 Elsasser 5/32 Brewer's Gold, 1/8 Northern Brewer, 1/16 Bullion, 1/32 Early Green, 1/32 German Aroma hop, 1/64 East Kent Golding, 1/128 Bavarian and 9/128 unknown. It was released in 2000.

name: Godiva
characteristics: The Godiva hop has tangerine, white grape and spice aromas. Sweet and smooth bittering characteristics.
heritage: Godiva is the daughter of Jester.

name: Golden Star
characteristics: Golden Star is grown commercially only in Japan as an aroma hop. It is very difficult to pick and shatters easily, particularly when grown seeded.
heritage: Golden Star is the offspring of Saaz and White Vine-OP conceived through open pollination. It was selected by Dr. Y. Mori from the Sapporo Brewery some time in the late 1960’s.  Some places mention this hop is the same as Sunbeam, but this has not yet been confirmed.

name: Golding
beer_styles: English Ale, ESB, Belgian Ale, Barleywine, Pale Ale
hop_sub: East Kent Golding, Fuggle, Willamette, Styrian Golding, Progress, Whitbread Golding Variety
characteristics: Golding hops have aroma descriptors that include delicate and sweet floral.
heritage: Golding descended from the original East Kent Golding. Also known as  Early Bird, Early Choice, Eastwell, and Mathon.

name: Green Bullet
beer_styles: Lager, Ales, Stout, Saison, Bock, ESB
hop_sub: Liberty, Hallertau, Mount Hood, Ultra, Crystal
characteristics:
Specific aroma descriptors of the Green Bullet hop includes black pepper, pine, plum and dried fruit characters; high levels of floral oil that complements and balances the piney resinous hop character. It has a resinous character reminiscent of Styrian Goldings, but layered with hints of musky Southern Hemisphere fruit.

It is traditionally considered a bittering variety for lagers, but also carries spicy characteristics typical of other Slovenian hop varieties.

This hop is regarded as a “workhorse” in its native New Zealand and is widely used there.
heritage: Green Bullet's parents were a Smoothcone and an open pollinated variety. It was developed in New Zealand DSIR Research Station. It was released in 1972.

name: Grungeist
characteristics: The unique combination of peach and passion fruit are equally balanced with a hint of lemon zest and floral essence. Grüngeist adds aromas and flavors of kiwi, overripe peach and dried lavender
heritage: Also known as Callista.

name: Hallertau (US)
hop_sub: Liberty, Mount Hood, Hallertau Mittelfruh, Crystal, Hallertau Tradition
characteristics: US Hallertau is mild and pleasant, yet spicy, with herbal and floral characteristics.
heritage: US Hallertau originates from the classic Hallertau variety of Germany.

name: Hallertau Blanc
beer_styles: IPA, Belgian Ale, Wheat Beer, Bretts, Pale Ale
characteristics: The Hallertau Blanc hop has moderate to strong featuring pineapple, gooseberry, white grape, fresh lemongrass, and passion fruit flavors and aromas. It is reminiscent of many recent Southern Hemisphere varieties, but with a cleaner, less dank profile.
heritage: Hallertau Blanc is a cross between a Cascade female and a Huell male. It was released to the public in 2012.

name: Hallertau Gold
hop_sub: Hallertau Mittelfruh, Tettnanger, East Kent Golding, Crystal, Mount Hood
characteristics: Hallertau Gold hops feature a pleasant and mild hoppiness with traditional notes of floral, herb, and spice.
heritage: This hop is a descendent of Hallertau and is often found under similar names.

name: Hallertau Mittelfrüh
beer_styles: Altbier, Belgian Ale, Pilsner, Bock, Lager, Wheat, Cask Ale
characteristics: Hallertau Mittelfrüh - or "Hall Mitt" - hops have a specific aroma descriptors include mild, yet spicy, with floral and citrus tones. Although it can be and is used throughout the boil, it is most prized for its fine, elegant aroma and flavor contribution. Mittelfrüh is, at least to some, the epitome of noble hops.
heritage: The Hallertau Mittelfrüh is the signature landrace variety of the Hallertau region in Bavaria, Germany. Sometimes it is called Hallertauer Mittelfruher.

name: Hallertau Taurus
characteristics: Hallertau Taurus is a hop with earthy aromas and hints of chocolate, banana, spice, pepper, curry. This hop has the highest xanthohumol content of any hop, which is a potent antioxidant.
heritage: Hallertau Taurus was released in 1995 from Hull breeding material. It was released in 1995.

name: Hallertau Tradition
hop_sub: Hallertau Mittelfruh, Tettnanger, East Kent Golding, Crystal, Mount Hood
characteristics: Hallertau Tradtion, or commonly referred to as just 'Tradition', adds an earthy and grassy character atop a nose of nectar fruits.
heritage: Registered in 1993, Tradition is a daughter of Hallertau Gold. It also has Hallertau Mittelfruher and Saaz in its lineage.

name: Harlequin
characteristics: The Harlequin hop excels when used in late and dry hopping but also offers smooth bittering characteristics.
heritage: This hop is a daughter of Godiva.

name: Harmonie
characteristics: Key flavors of the Harmonie hop include banana, creamy caramel, green tea and apricot.
heritage: Harmonie is a cross between Czech Bitter female (Saaz 50 %) and a selected Czech aroma male. It was registered as a variety in 2004.

name: Hartwick
characteristics: Hartwick hops have a piney and overripe pineapple aroma. GLH mentions that this hop's aroma is markedly different from their other Field Grade hops.
heritage: Hartwick hop is a "field grade" hop created by Great Lake Hops.

name: HBC 472
beer_styles: Pale Ale, IPA, Porter, Stout, IPL, Cream Ale
characteristics: The aroma of the HBC472 hop cones consists of floral, woody, earthy, and coconut. In beer, this hop delivers a surprising fruity note along with its distinctive coconut-woody character. When hopped aggressively in IPA style beers, citrus and grapefruit aromas rule, but a fascinating whiskey/bourbon and coconut character breaks into the background
heritage: Developed through the Hop Breeding Company (HBC) in the Yakima Valley, Washington and is the result of the open pollination of a wild American hop known as the subspecies neomexicanus.

name: HBC 586
characteristics: When used as a whirlpool or dry hop addition, HBC 586 brings a complex medley of tropical fruit flavors. The aroma of HBC 586 has been described as "a large medley of fruit flavors... Mango, guava, lychee, citrus, with slight sulfur and herbal notes."
heritage: HBC 586 is an experimental hop variety from the Hop Breeding Company, which is a joint venture between John I. Haas and Yakima Chief Ranches. HBC 586 is the result of a hybrid pollination of the mother YCR 21 and male #01239-2.

name: HBC 630
beer_styles: IPA, Wheat Ale, Golden Ale, NEIPA, Pale Ale, Lager
characteristics: HBC 630 when used as a whirlpool or dry hop addition has distinct cherry candy like – think cherry ludens – aroma, with notes of other stone fruits, raspberry candy, tropical fruits and citrus. A unique variety that offers a different aroma and flavor profile than most varieties available today.
heritage: HBC 630 is a granddaughter of Warrior. It resulted from a cross made in 2008 between HBC 364 and male parent 21-10-21.

name: HBC 692
beer_styles: Wheat Ale, Golden Ale, American Lager, Pale Ales, India Pale Lager, India Pale Ale, Session IPA, New England IPA, and Imperial IPA
characteristics:
HBC 692 exhibits grapefruit, floral, stone fruit, potpourri, woody, cream, pine, and resinous notes.

HBC 692 has been renamed Talus.
heritage: HBC 692 is an experimental hops cultivar developed by the Hop Breeding Company. HBC 692 resulted from a hybrid pollination of the cultivar Sabro-HBC 438 and open pollination.

name: Helga
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors include pleasant, noble characteristics.  Its brewing characteristics resemble that of Hallertau Mittelfrüher, however it demonstrates a forgiving and refined character in a variety of beer styles and hop applications.
heritage:
Helga was bred by Hop Products Australia from Hallertau Mittelfrüh and was formerly known as "Southern Hallertau". It was released to the public in 1986.

2017 was the last harvest of Helga by HPA, as it was retired.

name: Helios
characteristics:
Helios is described as low CoH, with a pleasant bitterness. It has a muted aroma with clean, soft accents. The aroma is resinous, spicy, and floral. For the farmers, it offers high yield and disease resistance. 

While it is listed as a dual purpose hop, it appears to be much closer to a bittering hop.
heritage: Helios™ is one of the most agronomically sustainable hop varieties grown today thanks to its unrivaled yield, powdery as well as downy mildew resistance, pleasant aroma and bitterness quality.

name: Herald
beer_styles: Dark Ale, Golden Ale, Pale Ale, ESB
characteristics: It is known for its clean bittering characteristics and fresh citrus flavors including orange and grapefruit.
heritage: Bred at Wye College and registered in 1996, Herald is a sister to Pioneer and Pilgrim hops.

name: Herkules
characteristics: Herkules hops have aroma descriptors that include robust hoppy with some citrus and melon.
heritage: Bred at the Hop Research Center in Hüll and released in 2006, Herkules is a daughter of Hallertau Taurus.

name: Hersbrucker
beer_styles: Dunkel, Strong Ale, Pilsner, Altbier, Weizenbock, Golden Ale, Marzen, Pale Ale, Wheat, Specialty Ale, Hefeweizen, Light Ale, Lager
characteristics: Hersbrucker hops have aroma descriptors that include hay, tobacco and orange.
heritage: Hersbrucker is a noble land variety originating from Southern Germany.

name: Horizon
characteristics: Horizon hops have aroma descriptors that include floral bouquet, citrus and spicy.
heritage: Horizon is a diploid hop and half-sister to Nugget.

name: HORT9909
characteristics: Hort9909 is a trial variety with pronounced forward citrus characters of lemon and lime, sweet hay and spices background in keeping with the selections breeding.
heritage: Hort9909 is triploid selection with “Hersbrucker Pure” parentage. Hersbrucker Pure itself being a cross of Hallertau Mittelfrüh, and Saaz and a wild German hop.

name: HPA-016
characteristics: HPA's newest proprietary flavor hop - tentatively called HPA-016 - is a big-hitting, fruit-forward flavor hop bursting with sweet mandarin, citrus peel and fresh pine needles.
heritage: After years of availability only in the Outback, it will be released to the rest of the world from 2021. HPA-016 was created by the HPA breeding program in 2004 and commercialized in 2020. Its ancestry is a cross pollination of high alpha Australian and North American hops.

name: Huell Melon
characteristics: Huell Melon has a pleasantly sweet and fruity character similar to honeydew melon with hints of strawberries.
heritage: Huell Melon hops were bred by the Hop Research Center in Hüll and is the daughter of Cascade and a Huell male. It was released in 2012.

name: Hüller Bitterer
characteristics: Hüller Bitterer has good bittering qualities, but production has decreased immensely over the years due to other hops having better storage.
heritage: The Huller Bitterer hop was descended from Northern Brewer and released in 1970.

name: Hydra
beer_styles: New England IPA, IPA, DIPA, Bohemian Pilsner
characteristics: Originally nicknamed "Befuddlement" for it's unique yet unidentifiable characteristics, Hydra has emerged with an incredible ability to amplify and juice up a brew.  Hydra can not only accentuate other hops characteristics but can easily shine solo.
readmore: Michigan Grown Hops: The Industry’s Best Kept Secret|https://beermaverick.com/michigan-grown-hops-tasting-trials-glh/

name: Idaho 7
hop_sub: Azacca, El Dorado, Cashmere, Citra, Idaho Gem, Amarillo, Chinook, Columbus
characteristics: Idaho 7 hops are known for their juicy tropical fruit and citrus flavors (think apricot, orange, red grapefruit, papaya) with big notes of resin-y pine and hints of floral and black tea. Use the Idaho 7 hop as a single hop or as a late addition. The high oil content make this ideal for dry hopping and whirlpool. Playfully known as "007: The Golden Hop"
heritage: The Idaho 7 hop was developed by Jackson Hop Farm in Wilder, ID. It was first released in 2015.
readmore: The Most Common Hops Used in IPAs|https://beermaverick.com/the-most-common-hops-used-in-ipas/

name: Idaho Gem
hop_sub: Azacca, El Dorado, Cashmere, Citra, Idaho 7, Cascade
characteristics:
Named after its home state, Idaho Gem has rich levels of sweet, fruit-forward aromatic oils make Idaho Gem optimal for late kettle additions or dry hopping in a wide variety of styles, whether alone or in a blend.

Idaho Gem shines with stone fruit, red berry, citrus, mojito, mint, and even powdered sugar aromas. Its flavors are soft and full with a remarkable smoothness, bright and forward impressions of fruit candy (think Juicy Fruit and Jolly Rancher) supported by citric grapefruit notes.
heritage: Idaho Gem was found by Gooding Farms in Parma

name: Independence
beer_styles: Pilsner, Lager, Blonde Ale, Pale Ale
characteristics: Paying homage to the classic hops, Independence blend merges old world with new world spirit giving a truly American aroma and flavor profile. Bringing forth tobacco, earthy, pine and grassy notes with citrus and herbs to support the aroma profile, Independence symbolizes the pioneer spirit carried through the many generations of growers in the Pacific Northwest.
heritage: Independence is a proprietary blend of hops by Yakima Chief Hops.

name: Jarrylo
beer_styles: Wheat Beer, Saison, Pale Ale, Belgian Ale
characteristics: Jarrylo hops have aroma descriptors that include strong lemon lime, orange, pear, banana and mild spice. Some reviewers also notice a clean bright white wine aroma.
heritage: Jarrylo's parentage includes Summit mother and a ADHA 75-2 father.

name: Jester
characteristics: Blackcurrant, Grapefruit, Resinous
heritage: Jester is a seedling of Cascade. It was commercially released in 2013.

name: Junga
beer_styles: Imperial Stout,Porter, Pale Ale, Brown Ale, California Common, IPA
characteristics: While some call Junga a dual-purpose hop, it is mainly used for bittering. Has limited potential for aroma or dry hopping use. Sometimes Iunga or Lunga. This varietal is high in alpha acids (10-13%) and most frequently utilized for bittering. However, if used for aroma, Junga hops give blackcurrant, grapefruit, and spice notes. Junga is comparable to German Magnum; not much taste but a great bittering hop.
heritage: Bred by the IUNG Puławy from Northern Brewer, Marynka and Saaz stock.

name: Kazbek
beer_styles: Lagers, Pale Ales, Wheats, Belgian Ales
characteristics: Kazbek can show a dominant bright citrus note of lemon or grapefruit, but never obscures the quintessential mixture of floral and earthy spice of its Saaz parent.
heritage: Kazbek was bred from Saaz and a wild Russian landrace hop from the Caucasus.

name: Kohatu
beer_styles: Belgian Ale, Wheat Beer, Blonde Ale, IPA, Pale Ale
characteristics: Kohatu hops have aroma descriptors include fresh and intense tropical fruit characters and an excellent finish and bitterness.
heritage: Released in 2011, Kohatu is a descendant of Hallertau Mittelfrüh.

name: Lambic
characteristics: Floral and Fruity. These are aged hops that are perfect for making sours.
heritage: A triploid hybrid of the English Fuggle released in 1976 from the USDA breeding program.

name: Lawton
characteristics: Lawton hops have pungent, woody & earthy aromas, not citrusy at all, but totally dank.
heritage: Lawton is an unknown feral-type hop collected in Michigan, 2012 by GLH. Great Lake Hops refer to Lawton as a "field grade" hop.

name: Lemondrop
beer_styles: American Pale Ale,Saison,Cream Ale,Hybrid Beer,IPA,Trappist, Belgian Ale,Wheat Beer
hop_sub: Cascade, Motueka, Liberty, Mandarina Bavaria, Centennial
characteristics: Lemondrop hops offer strong citrus, floral, fruity, herbal, lemon, mint, green tea, light melon aromas.
heritage: The Lemondrop hop is a cross between Cascade and USDA 19058 male. it was bred in 2001.

name: Liberty
beer_styles: American Lager, German Lager, Pilsner, Bock, Kolsch, Wheat
characteristics: Liberty hops have aroma descriptors that include noble, delicate, floral bouquet and spice.  It displays mild floral and spice characteristics with some subtle citrus notes.
heritage:
Bred in 1983, Liberty is an extension of the Hallertau hop family. It is a half-sister to Ultra,
Mt. Hood and Crystal. It is a triploid seedling of the German Hallertau variety.

name: Limbus
characteristics: This is a Polish dwarf aroma hop.
heritage: Daughter of Northern Brewer and was released in 1996.

name: Loral
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, Lager, Pilsner, Wheats, Saison
hop_sub: Glacier, Nugget, Strisselspalt, Tardif De Bourgogne
characteristics: Loral hops have a very pleasant, floral, peppery, citrus aroma with some dark fruit character. The floral, fruity, citrus, character tends to be more prominent in a beer made with this hop, while the qualities of earthy and herbal notes stay more subdued.
heritage: Loral's father is Nugget and its mother is Glacier. Loral has a noble French pedigree on its mother’s side, with Glacier being the granddaughter of Tardif De Bourgogne. It was released publically in May 2016.
statistics: In 2019, Loral was the 33rd most produced hop in the United States.

name: Lotus
characteristics: Lotus is described  as having orange, vanilla, berry, and tropical fruits and was jokingly called orange creamsicle. This is a proprietary hop from Hopsteiner formerly called Experimental #06297.
heritage: Lotus hops have a diverse parentage, including 50% Eastern Gold (a Japanese variety), 25% Apollo, Cascade, and USDA 19058.

name: Lubelska
characteristics: Lubelska hops have a distinctive and very refreshing characters of spice and flowers (magnolia and lavender).
heritage: Lubelska is a cultivar of Saaz, sometimes referred to as Lublin or Lublelski.

name: Lumberjack
beer_styles: Pilsner, Lagers, Pale Ale, Belgian Ale
characteristics: BC Hop Co.’s proprietary hop, Lumberjack, aroma can be described as predominately bitter with notes of melon, sweet citrus, and spices like clove and allspice.  A slight earthiness softens this hop, producing a pleasant mouth-feel in the beer.

name: Mackinac
characteristics: This super-aroma hop has proven to execute wide utility and flavorful new beer styles that are trending toward these aromas and flavors. The moderately low cohumulone offers brewers a smoother brewing profile with excellent foam characteristics. It has gained traction and praise across the United States since and has not only increased in demand but cannot be planted quickly enough to fulfill requests.
heritage: Mackinac was released from the GLH trials and breeding program in 2014.
readmore: Michigan Grown Hops: The Industry’s Best Kept Secret|https://beermaverick.com/michigan-grown-hops-tasting-trials-glh/

name: Magnat
characteristics: Magnat hops have key flavors that include citrus, woody, chamomile and lemon. Despite being classical bitterness aroma it has quite high linalool content, so it can enrich the aroma with the notes of lilac and flowers.
heritage: Magnat is the daughter of Magnum, and has a pedigree of Lubelski and a Yugoslavian male. It was released in 2012.

name: Magnum (GR)
hop_sub: Magnum (US), Nugget, Taurus, Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ
characteristics: German Magnum hops have aroma descriptors that include apple and pepper. It provides clean bitterness and subtle citrus flavors.
heritage: Magnum is the daughter of Galena and the unnamed male German hop 75/5/3. It was released in 1980.

name: Magnum (US)
beer_styles: Ale, Lager, Stout, Pale Ale, IPA
hop_sub: Galena, Magnum (GR), Nugget, Columbus, Horizon, Northdown, Northern Brewer (US)
characteristics: US Magnum is a bittering hop with an excellent bittering profile and a nice, hoppy, floral aroma and subtle characters of citrus.
heritage: The original German-grown Magnum was released in 1980 and hails from the German Hop Institute in Hull.

name: Mandarina Bavaria
beer_styles: American Pale Ale, Sours, New England IPA, IPA, Saison, Black Ale, Brett
characteristics: Mandarina Bavaria hops are tropical, fruity and sweet with notes of tangerine and citrus. It is commonly used to balances earthy and herbal hops. This hop is very versatile and plays well in any beer style that would benefit from its distinctive fruity character. Also consider using as a dry hop/late addition in IPAs.
heritage: Mandarina Bavaria is a daughter of Cascade, and Hallertau Blanc and Hüll Melon males (94/045/001 x wild PM). It was released in 2012.

name: Marco Polo
characteristics: Key flavors of the Marco Polo hop include lemon, gooseberry, oregano and elder flower.
heritage: Marco Polo is derived from Columbus.
statistics: It makes up about 8% of China’s growing acreage.

name: Marynka
characteristics: Key flavors of the Marynka hop include hay, licorice, lemon, grapefruit and aniseed. While it is primarily used for bittering, it can also be used to provide a strong earthy, herbal aroma.
heritage: Marynka is a daughter of Brewer's Gold.

name: McKenzie
characteristics: McKenzie hops are the first variety to be released by the West Coast Hop Breeding Company out of Oregon. McKenzie™ was specifically bred to be grown in Oregon and highlight the area's terroir. McKenzie is "bright and fruity" with grapefruit, lemon, nectarine, and melon aromas that is backed by pine, resin, and wood. Fits really well in the classic West Coast IPA or American Pale Ale, but also plays well in fruit-forward and Hazy IPAs.

name: Medusa
characteristics: Medusa delivers strong flavor and aroma characteristics of intense guava, melon, apricot and citrus fruit. Also has intense lemon-lime flavors with subtle alfalfa and peach flavors.
heritage: Medusa is a native North American Humulus lupus var. neomexicanus recovered from the wild in the mountains of New Mexico.

name: Melba
characteristics: Melba hops have passion fruit, grapefruit, citrus and summery aroma characteristics. When used as an early addition, it is said to impart a clean and somewhat spicy bitterness. When used as a flavor or aroma addition though, properties of passion fruit, grapefruit and citrus come to the fore.
heritage: Melba comes from the Ellerslie breeding program.

name: Meridian
beer_styles: Belgian Ale, Pale Ale, Lager, IPA, Wheat
characteristics: The Meridian hop has aromas that include citrus (lemon), mixed berry, tropical fruit, and spearmint. This hop can also be combined with more aggressive hops in IPAs to brighten the overall impression of the beer and lend a seductive finish.
heritage: A chance discovery in 2011, this hop was accidentally grown by Indie Hops at Goschie Farms, Oregon, when they were trying to recreate and grow Columbia hops, which itself descends from the Willamette hop.

name: Merkur
beer_styles: IPA, Lager, Belgian Ale, Pilsner, Stout
hop_sub: Magnum (GR), Magnum (US), Hallertau Taurus, Hallertau Tradition
characteristics: Merkur has aroma descriptors that include sugar, pineapple and mint.  In some dual-purpose applications, Merkur displays subtle earth and citrus notes.
heritage: Merkur is a cross between Magnum and the German experimental variety 81/8/13. It was released in 2001.

name: Michigan Copper
beer_styles: American Pale Ale, India Pale Ale, Pale Ale, Wheat, Belgians, Lagers, Stouts
characteristics: Michigan Copper is described as a vigorous super-aroma hop with very fragrant floral and tropical fruit aromas and flavors. Rich in fruit and candy notes, this hop has been likened to Hawaiian fruit punch, black cherry, red hard candy, and hibiscus resin.
heritage: Michigan Copper was released from the Great Lakes Hops (GLH) trials and breeding program in 2014.
readmore: Michigan Grown Hops: The Industry’s Best Kept Secret|https://beermaverick.com/michigan-grown-hops-tasting-trials-glh/

name: Millennium
characteristics: Millennium hops have aroma descriptors that include resin, floral, toffee and pear.
heritage: Millennium is the daughter of Nugget, and was released in 2000.

name: Mimosa
characteristics: Little is known at this time about the new Mimosa hop. It was created in 2019, and not yet available commercially.
heritage: Created in 2019.

name: Minstrel
characteristics: The Minstrel hop has herbal, orange, and spiced berry aromas. It also has a mild bitterness, that is not unlike Challenger.
heritage: Minstrel is a seedling of Sovereign, dwarf and was planted in 2006 as part of the Charles Faram Hop Development Program. It was released commercially in 2012. In 2016, breeders started to see some confusing symptoms of Verticillium wilt, and by 2020, the decision was to halt production of this hop due to its poor disease resistance.

name: Mistral
beer_styles: IPA, Saison, Pale Ale, Belgian Ale, Pilsner
characteristics: When it is used in the kettle, the bitterness of the Mistral hop is very clean and sweet and not harsh. It is completely unique with a character that would be impossible to replicate without blending multiple varieties.  It has a melon and citrus-like character and also can have a Muscat grape or wine-like character and even a hint of pear. Ideal for late additions and dry hopping.
heritage: Developed by the Comptoir Agricole breeding program in Alsace, France. Released to the US in 2019.

name: Monroe
characteristics: The Monroe hop starts out mild and smooth, then adds key flavors like strawberry, cherry, plum, passion fruit and red currant. Wonderful raspberry notes supplemented with orange syrup aromas. This is a very unique hop that is unlike any others. It is smooth and restrained in the German tradition, but with plenty of red-fruit character.
heritage: The Monroe hop variety was derived from a wild American hop.

name: Mosaic
beer_styles: American Pale Ale, IPA, Double IPA, Stout
characteristics:
Mosaic offers a unique and complex blend of floral, tropical, fruity, and earthy characteristics that translate very favorably into several different styles of beer, although it is primarily used in IPAs.

Mosaic's complex flavor and aroma profile led to its name. This hop is most noted for its blueberry and tropical aromas, but is also known to have citrus, grassy, pine, spice, tangerine, papaya, rose, earthy, floral (blossoms), and bubble gum descriptors. 

The aroma/flavor descriptors for the wildly popular Mosaic hop commonly include fruity (blueberry), citrus (tangerine, grapefruit), tropical (mango, guava), floral, and earthy. The combination of these aroma/flavor descriptors with the relatively high alpha-acid levels allows this hop to serve a dual purpose, as both a bittering and flavor/aroma hop in the brewing process. Ultimately, Mosaic provides flavor and aroma profiles in beer that simply cannot be accomplished with other hop cultivars.

Mosaic hops are one of the most used - and recognizable - hop varieties in use today. It can be used in many different beer styles, but will usually be found in IPAs and pale ales. 

As for growing properties of this hop, Mosaic has a tight-knit appearance with a compact cone. Mosaic has a unique aroma combined with high α-acid content, powdery mildew tolerance, and exceptional yield.
heritage: Mosaic is the daughter of the Simcoe (YCH 14) hop and a Nugget-derived male which had a linage including Tomahawk, Brewers Gold, Early Green, and an unknown variety. It was released to the craft brewing scene in 2012 by the Hop Breeding Company (HBC).
