name: Motueka
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, European Lager, Belgian Ales, English Ale, Pilsner, Mexican Lager
characteristics:
The Motueka hop has specific aroma descriptors include distinctive fresh crushed citrus, mojito lime character, lively lemon and lime tones with background hints of tropical fruit. It also has fresh herbal notes (basil, rosemary) and hints of dried orchard fruit around the edges. Motueka was originally bred as a dual-purpose hop because of its balanced bitterness and new world “noble” type aroma. 

The weight of oil to alpha integrates it fully with higher gravity types to balance both malt sweetness and body. Extremely versatile in the brewery.
heritage: New Zealand Motueka as released in 1998 from Hort Research. It was bred from a cross between Saaz and a New Zealand breeding selection. Initially called Belgian Saaz, or abbreviated to 'B' Saaz.
readmore: Southern Hemisphere Hops: Superlatives Edition|https://beermaverick.com/southern-hemisphere-hops-superlatives-edition/
beer_examples: Hill farmstead Single Hop Motueka Pale Ale|https://hillfarmstead.com/beer/motueka-single-hop-pale-ale/;Other Half Motueka Daydream|https://otherhalfbrewing.com/beer/motueka-daydream/

name: Mount Hood
beer_styles: Ale, Lager, Pilsner, Bock, Altbier, Munich Helles, Wheat
hop_sub: Hallertau, Hersbrucker, Liberty, Crystal, Hallertau Mittelfruh
characteristics: Mount Hood hops have aroma descriptors that include herbal, pungent and spicy notes.
heritage: Mount Hood is a triploid seedling of the German Hallertauer variety. It is the daughter of Hallertauer Mittelfrueh and sister to Liberty, Crystal and Ultra. It was released in 1989.

name: Mount Rainier
characteristics: Mount Rainier hops have aroma descriptors that include noble, licorice and floral bouquet. It also has a hint of citrus and spice.
heritage: Mount Rainier is a cross between Magnum and USDA 19085M (from Oregon State University). It was made in 1994 in Corvallis, OR and released in 2009.

name: Moutere
characteristics: Moutere hops have intensely tropical and fruity with dominant grapefruit and passion fruit notes. It also has undertones of spry hay, earthy baking spice, and resinous pine.
heritage: A triploid variety bred from Southern Cross and a New Zealand male, Moutere was released in 2015.

name: Multihead
characteristics: Multihead is a neomexicanus breed so named for its tendency to produce dual cones. Low alpha acid levels and high oil content make this hop perfect for packing flavor and aroma into your beer. Known for imparting intense tropical flavors of melon, guava, apricot and citrus. Although primarily used as a late addition, Multihead is known to contribute a mellow, peachy character when added early in the boil.
heritage: Also known as Medusa hops.

name: Mystic
hop_sub: Barbe Rouge, Styrian Dragon, Huell Melon
characteristics: Mystic is a lower alpha acid variety that provides clean bittering in lighter styles, but is mostly used as an aroma variety because of its fruit-forward character. Mystic is not as big and bold as fellow UK variety Harlequin, but Mystic offers a unique combination of black currant, citrus, and passion fruit. Mystic is the daughter of Jester and was selected primarily for its amazing aroma, but also because of disease resistance and yield potential.
heritage: Daughter of Jester that was selected for its aroma, disease resistance, and yield.

name: Nectaron
beer_styles: IPA, NEIPA, Strong Pale Ale, XPA
characteristics: Nectaron has intense tropical fruit and citrus aroma characters evident during selection. Over successive commercial brewing trials in styles such as Strong Pale Ales and India Pale Ales, Nectaron has displayed high levels of tropical fruit characters of pineapple and passionfruit as well as stone fruit (peach) and citrus (grapefruit).
heritage: Nectaron is a triploid aroma type developed by New Zealand’s Plant & Food Research. Nectaron (HORT4337) is a full sister to Waimea and daughter of Pacific Jade. This hop variety was first bred in 2004, expanded in 2016-2017 and finally released commercially in 2020.
readmore: Southern Hemisphere Hops: Superlatives Edition|https://beermaverick.com/southern-hemisphere-hops-superlatives-edition/

name: Nelson Sauvin
characteristics:
The Nelson Sauvin hop aroma descriptors include distinctive white wine characters along with fruity flavors, such as fresh crushed gooseberry and grape. The essential oil profile displays characteristics of fresh crushed gooseberries, which is a descriptor often used for the grape variety Sauvignon Blanc, which is the reason for this variety's name.

This is a hop that may require efficient application in the brew house, this truly unique dual-purpose variety can be used to produce big punchy Ales as well as subtle aroma driven Lagers. The fruitiness may be a little overpowering for the uninitiated, however those with a penchant for bold hop character will find several applications for this true brewer's hop.
heritage: Nelson Sauvin is a triploid variety developed at Hort Research, Riwaka Research Centre from a Smoothcone and NZ male cross. It was first released in 2000.
readmore: Southern Hemisphere Hops: Superlatives Edition|https://beermaverick.com/southern-hemisphere-hops-superlatives-edition/

statistics: In 2019, 1,041 metric tonnes of Nelson Sauvin hops were harvested, making it the most grown hop in New Zealand.|Many hop farms in New Zealand are at least 50% Nelson Sauvin hops. Many farmers are able to retire off this hop.

name: Newport
hop_sub: Brewer's Gold, Fuggle, Galena, Magnum (GR), Magnum (US), Nugget
characteristics: Newport hops have aroma descriptors that include earth, citrus, wine and balsamic. It contains high alpha acid, co-humulone and myrcene content, offering more distinct aroma characteristics than its parents.
heritage: Newport is the offspring of Magnum and a USDA male variety. It was released in 1992 and bred by Oregon State University.

name: Nobility Blend
characteristics: Nobility was developed specifically for lager brewing, with a focus on a balanced noble-style hop character.  Primary aromas and flavors of citrus (lemon, orange), lemongrass, green tea, and herbaceous, with a mixture of floral and spice.
heritage: After multiple rounds of brewing trials at the Rahr Technical Center, it was added to the BSG Hop Solutions portfolio in 2020.

name: Northdown
beer_styles: Heavy Ale, Porter, Barley Wine, Stout, Bock
hop_sub: Northern Brewer (US), Northern Brewer (GR), Target, Challenger, Admiral
characteristics: Northdown hops have aroma descriptors that include pleasant spice, cedar and pine characteristics with hints of floral and berry flavors.  It is known for its fresh, yet rich flavor.
heritage: Northdown hops were developed at Wye College in 1970 as offspring of Northern Brewer and Challenger and an aunt to Target.

name: Northern Brewer (GR)
hop_sub: Northern Brewer (US), Brewers Gold, Chinook, Perle (US), Perle (GR)
characteristics: German Northern Brewer hops have aroma descriptors that include mint, pine and grass. It displays pleasant pine and mint characteristics most in dual purpose brewing applications.
heritage: Northern Brewer was originally developed in England in 1934 but is now largely grown in Germany.

name: Northern Brewer (US)
beer_styles: Porter, Ale, Kolsch, Munich Helles, ESB, German Lager, English Pale Ale
hop_sub: Galena, Perle (US), Magnum (GR), Magnum (US), Chinook, Northern Brewer (GR)
characteristics: US Northern Brewer hops have aroma descriptors that include evergreen, wood and mint.   US Northern Brewer contains slightly higher alpha acids and high myrcene oil content than the German variety, resulting in a herbal, wood and peppery aroma. It is suitable for any stage of the brewing process.
heritage: Northern Brewer hop is a German cross between a Canterbury Golding and 'OB21' - a male seedling of Brewers Gold. It is mainly grown in the US and Germany.

name: Nugget
beer_styles: Ale, Stout, Barleywine, Saison, Biere de Garde, IPA
hop_sub: Galena, Magnum (US), Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ
characteristics: Nugget is an excellent high-alpha bittering hop for most craft styles that can also be used for its mild, pleasant and herbal aroma notes.
heritage: Nugget is a cross between Brewers Gold and a high alpha-acid male. It was released in 1983.
readmore: Five Hop Varieties That Don’t Get Enough Love|https://beermaverick.com/five-hop-varieties-that-dont-get-enough-love/

name: NZH-107
characteristics:
NZH-107 is a proprietary experimental hop blend developed jointly between New Zealand Hops, Ltd. and Brewers Supply Group and showcases intense tropical fruit (guava/passion fruit) and citrus (grapefruit/lime) aroma characteristics.

NZH-107 is an excellent hop solution for many applications from first kettle to dry hop additions. Given the big character of NZH-107, early trials are focused on intensely hop-forward styles like IPAs, pale ales, and dry-hopped pale lagers.
heritage: NZH-107 is a proprietary experimental hop blend developed jointly between New Zealand Hops, Ltd. and Brewers Supply Group. NZH-107 was created as part of the BSG Hop Solutions Program and is exclusively sold through BSG.

name: Old Mission
characteristics: Noted aromas of the Old Mission hop includes citrus, light pine, tea spice, herbal, pepper & tartness.
heritage: Old Mission was created as a "field grade" hop by Great Lake Hops (GLH).

name: Olicana
characteristics: Olicana hops exhibit flavors and aromas of grapefruit, mango and passion fruit.
heritage: Olicana is a sister of Jester. It was commercially released in 2014. This variety was planted in 2009 in Herefordshire/Worcestershire and released commercially in 2014.

name: Olympic
characteristics: Olympic hops are primarily used as a bittering hop, however some subtle citrus and spice aroma characteristics have been noted.
heritage:
Released for commercial production in 1983, Olympic is a descendant of Brewer’s Gold,
Fuggle and East Kent Golding.

name: Omega
characteristics: The Omega hop has been described as “pleasantly European in style”.
heritage: Omega was developed at Wye College with Challenger female and an unknown English variety as parents. It has disappointing farm yields, thus it is not grown much anymore.

name: Opal
beer_styles: Belgian Ale, Hefeweizen, Helles, Lager, Pilsner, Wheats
hop_sub: East Kent Golding, Styrian Golding, Tettnanger
characteristics: Opal hops have aroma descriptors that include spice, pepper, citrus and an even dispersal of fruity, floral and herbal.  It is specifically known for its sweet and spicy characteristics, providing subtle pepper and clean citrus flavors.
heritage: Opal is a daughter of Hallertau Gold.

name: Orion
beer_styles: German Ale, Lager, Helles, Dunkel, Pilsner
characteristics: Orion hops have pleasant European bittering and aroma qualities. It is not widely grown, and is very hard to find outside of Germany.
heritage: Orion is a cross between German Perle and 70/10/15M, released in the 1980s. It is a half-sister to Challenger.

name: Pacific Crest
characteristics: This blend connects the classic noble varieties with an American influence. Bringing together grassy, earthy and tobacco characteristics with mild floral, spicy, herbal and pine.  The Pacific Crest Blend is a specifically formulated hop blend that targets the hallmark profiles and terpene ratios of noble varieties.
heritage: This is a proprietary blend of hops

name: Pacific Gem
characteristics: The Pacific Gem has specific aroma descriptors that include spicy black pepper and berry fruit aroma characteristics. Some brewers have noted delicate blackberry, floral, pine or oak tones as well.
heritage: The ancestry of the Pacific Gem hop includes Smoothcone, Californian Late Cluster, and Fuggle. It was released to the public in 1987.

name: Pacific Jade
characteristics: Pacific Jade hops have descriptors that include fresh citrus and complex spice characters with some bold herbal aromas and hints of black pepper. It is suited for use as a bittering hop but also delivers bold aroma characteristics.
heritage: The Pacific Jade hop is a triploid high-alpha variety bred by HortResearch Centre in Riwaka, NZ. It was released in 2004. Pacific Jade's ancestry includes New Zealand First Choice (itself related to the Late Cluster) and an Old Line Saazer male.

name: Pacific Sunrise
characteristics: Pacific Sunrise features favorable bittering properties and a pleasant piney, woodsy aroma. Lemon and orange citrus drive this hop-forward variety, with tropical hits of melon and mango riding shotgun. Stone fruit and jammy sweetness support the initial front line aromatics alongside pleasant floral notes that help round out the overall profile. Subtle, yet notable hints of pine & berry refract through the aroma with lingering bits of hay and herbs that stay in their lane and are not distracting.
heritage: Pacific Sunrise has parentage that includes Late Cluster, Fuggle, a European male and a NZ male. It was released by the New Zealand Hort Research program in 2000.

name: Pacifica
beer_styles: Pale Ale, Kölsch, Altbier, Belgian Ale
characteristics: Pacifica has aroma descriptors that include a signature citrus orange zest marmalade; classic Hallertau characteristics with some floral notes. Moderate and highly pleasant. Orange marmalade and delicate citrus aromas come from this hop, blended with a very Old World Hallertau-esque warmth. It also has new-mown hay and honey with tangy herbal and sweet floral notes.
heritage: Pacifica was bred by HortResearch and has noble European ancestry through an open cross with Hallertau Mittelfruh. It was released in 1994.

name: Pahto
characteristics: When used as a bittering addition hop, Pahto provides a very neutral flavor to beer, and a pleasant, smooth bitterness. The aroma profile of the hop cone is described as herbal, earthy, woody, and resinous with some fruit.
heritage: The mother of Pahto is a breeding line of the HBC with similar characteristics. The father is from the breeding program at Wye College in Kent, England.

statistics:
Pahto is 2nd in the amount of new acreage dedicated to it between 2014 and 2019|
In 2019, Pahto was the 7th most grown hop in the United States

name: Palisade
beer_styles: American IPA, Saison, Wheat, Brett, English Lager
characteristics: Palisade hops have aroma descriptors that include apricot, grass and clean floral characteristics.
heritage: Palisade was bred by Yakima Chief Ranch via open pollination.

name: Paradigm
beer_styles: Vienna Lager, IPA, New England IPA
characteristics: ​Paradigm is the proud beginning of an entire movement, an industry shift that may very well revitalize what it means to commercially grow hops for Midwest Hop growers. Aromas Identified by brewers include Herbal, Peach, Pear, Dank, Tropical, Cotton Candy, Mint, Papaya, Spice, Chamomile, Catty, Spicy Berry, Jolly Rancher, Bubble Gum, Lily and Rich Melon.
readmore: Michigan Grown Hops: The Industry’s Best Kept Secret|https://beermaverick.com/michigan-grown-hops-tasting-trials-glh/

name: Pekko
characteristics: The Pekko hop is an intriguing mix of flavors like mint, pear, citrus, cucumber and floral aspects. Pekko's complex and clean characteristics of floral, citrus, and mint lend itself to many styles of beer.
heritage: Pekko's lineage includes an open pollinated male with ADHA 538 as the female.

name: Perle (GR)
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors of the German Perle hop include herbal and spicy with delicate floral, fruit and mint tones.
heritage: German Perle hops were bred at Hull from English Northern Brewer stock and released in 1978.

name: Perle (US)
beer_styles: Pale Ale, Porter, Stout, Lager, Weizen, Altbier, Barleywine, Kolsch, Wheat, Pilsner
hop_sub: Perle (GR), Northern Brewer (US), Northern Brewer (GR), Cluster, Galena
characteristics: US Perle hops have aroma descriptors that include floral and spicy.  Perle is known for adding a traditional, German-like quality to beer.
heritage: Perle is a German variety bred in Hüll resulting from a cross between Northern Brewer and 63/5/27M. It was released in 1978.

name: Petit Blanc
characteristics: Petit Blanc hops have an intensive fruity flavor. Common descriptors include Papaya/Melon, Cherry, Lilac, Pina Colada, Lemon Blossom, Green Tea, Bees Wax.
heritage: Old, nearly lost French variety, origin unknown, formerly grown in the French region “Champagne-Ardenne” close to the burgundy region. Found in the backyard of an old former hop grower.

name: Petoskey
characteristics: Petoskey hops are "field grade" hops that come from Great Lake Hops (GLH).
heritage: Petoskey hops have Cluster heritage, but their cones are not shaped like typical Clusters.

name: Phoenix
beer_styles: English Ale, Porter, Stout, ESB, Bitter
characteristics: Phoenix hops have chocolate, pine, molasses and floral aromas.
heritage: Phoenix is a seedling of Yeoman, developed at Horticulture Research International (HRI), Wye College in the UK and released for general cultivation in 1996.

name: Pilgrim
beer_styles: English Pale Ale, Nut Brown Ale, Stout
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors of the Pilgrim hop include distinct fruit and spice characteristics with pleasant lemon, grapefruit, pear and berry flavors. Pilgrim is a bittering hop and it has a full-bodied, refreshing and rounded bitterness. However, Pilgrim is also not too overpowering, so it’s a great hop to use if you want a very versatile yet robust flavor.
heritage: Pilgrim is a half-sister to ‘First Gold, having the same father. Its mother is from breeding lines derived from Wye Challenger and Wye Target. It was released in 2001 by the Horticulture Research International at Wye College.

name: Pilot
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors of the Pilot hop include lemon, spice and marmalade. Pilot has a wonderfully refreshing, clean and crisp bittering quality.
heritage: Pilot was bred at Horticulture Research International (HRI) Wye College in the UK and released in 2001.

name: Pioneer
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors of the Pioneer hop include pleasant citrus (lemon and grapefruit) tones and hints of herbal, cedar flavors. Pioneer's bittering characteristics are crisp, refreshing and clean.
heritage: Bred at Wye College from Omega and is a sister to Herald. It was released in 1996.

name: Polaris
characteristics: Polaris hops have aroma descriptors that include mint, pineapple and menthol.
heritage: Polaris is a cross between 94/075/758 and 97/060/720 (which is a derivative of Huell material).

name: Premiant
characteristics: Premiant hops have aroma descriptors that include pleasant, mild and fruity. The unusually low content of cohumulone in combination with other typical characteristics of this variety contributes to the neutral character of bitterness.
heritage: Premiant  was created by crossing of bitter varieties with Czech Saaz aroma varieties.

name: Pride of Ringwood
beer_styles: British Ale, Australian Ale, Australian Lager
characteristics: Pride of Ringwood hops have aroma descriptors that include pronounced and pleasant with cedar, oak and herbal tones.
heritage: Pride of Ringwood was bred in Australia from an English variety Pride of Kent and released in 1965.

name: Progress
beer_styles: Cask Ale, English Ale, Scottish Ale
characteristics: Progress hops have aroma descriptors that include grass, mint, sweet, honey, and blackcurrant.
heritage: Progress was developed at Wye College in 1951 as a daughter of Whitbred Golding  and O.B.79, making it cousin to Target.

name: Prussian
characteristics: GLH says the Prussian hop has lemony citrus aromas with piney notes.
heritage: Prussian is a "field grade" hop created by Great Lake Hops.

name: Pulawski
characteristics: Puławski has a pungent and complex aroma with white grapes, spices, clove and ripe papaya. Another promising new wave aroma hop.
heritage: New release from IUNG Institute in 2012. It is daugher bred from Lubelski family.

name: Rakau
beer_styles: IPA, Belgian Ale, Pale Ale, Lager
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors for the Rakau hop include stone fruit and fig characteristics; fresh orchard fruits, specifically apricot with some resinous pine needle characteristics are noted. Sometimes it is misspelled as Raku.
heritage: Hort Research released Rakau in 2007.

name: Relax
characteristics: With cornflower and the aroma of alpine meadows to lemongrass and hibiscus, Relax hops have a refreshing profile. When a cold extraction with this variety is completed, notes of honeydew melon are present.
heritage: Relax hops come from a Huell breeding line.

name: Riwaka
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors of the Riwaka hop includes powerfully fueled tropical passion fruit with grapefruit and citrus characters. This variety has a higher than average oil content, almost double that of its Saazer parent.
heritage: Riwaka (AKA D-Saaz) is a triploid variety crossed with an old line Saazer and specially developed New Zealand breeding selections. It was released in 1996.
beer_examples: Hill farmstead Riwaka Single Hop Pale Ale|https://hillfarmstead.com/beer/riwaka-single-hop-pale-ale/

name: Saaz (CZ)
characteristics: Czech Republic Saaz has aroma descriptors that include mild with pleasant earthy, herbal and floral overtones.
heritage:
Saaz is a staple variety for brewers and dates back more than 700 years. A Czech Republic landrace variety, Saaz is the most classic “noble” aroma hop with
longstanding and strong traditions

name: Saaz (US)
hop_sub: Saaz (CZ), Polish Lublin, Sterling, Tettnang, Sorachi Ace
characteristics:
Saaz is a noble hop variety, and is considered one of the oldest known. Saaz hops have aromas that include earthiness with a mild spicy flavor to it. Saaz hops - regardless of the country they are grown in - are mostly only used for its aromas due to its low alpha acid content, which results in a delicate bitterness when added at the end of the boil.

Noble hops like Saaz, are aroma varieties that have an extensive history behind them. The term ‘noble’ has been used since the 1980’s and refers to hops that have been used in brewing beer for centuries.
heritage: Saaz hops were bred from the original centuries-old Czech Republic landrace variety. Czech Saaz hops are of the same lineage, but are grown in the Czech Republic, whereas this variety is grown in the US. The name Saaz is based on the Czech city of Žatec.
readmore: All About Noble Hops|https://beermaverick.com/types-of-hops-aroma-noble-bittering-dual-purpose/
statistics: Saaz hops accounted for more than two thirds of total 2009 hop production in the Czech Republic.

name: Saaz Late
characteristics: Beer hopped with Saaz Late is close to beers hopped with the traditional Saaz variety, but the lingering of the bitterness is slightly less. The character of the bitterness is somewhat less with greater astringency.
heritage: Saaz Late was a selection from progenies of F1 generation after parental combination of developed breeding material with origin in Saaz. Saaz Late was released 2010.

name: Sabro
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, Fruit Beer, Porter, Stout
characteristics: The Sabro hop has complex and distinct fruit and citrus aromas. It also has a unique blend of tangerine, coconut, tropical and stone fruit flavors underscored by hints of cedar and mint. Sabro proves to be a strongly expressive hop that translates its flavor incredibly well into beer.
heritage: Sabro is the result of a unique cross-pollination of a female Neomexicanus hop. It was released in 2018.
statistics:
Sabro hops are primarily grown in Washington state.|
In 2019, the Sabro hop was 20th in total production, coming in at 1,167,100 pounds harvested.

name: Samba
characteristics: Complex aroma characterized by juicy tropical fruits (pineapple, mango), stone fruits, candy, and orange tangerine.
heritage: This hop blend is the first proprietary release in the BSG Hop Solutions Program.

name: Santiam
beer_styles: Lager, Pilsner, Belgian Tripel, Kolsch, Bock, Helles
characteristics: Santiam hops have aroma descriptors that include black pepper, floral and spice.
heritage: Santiam's parent varietals are Swiss Tettnanger, German Hallertau Mittelfruher and Cascade. It was rleased in 1997 by the USDA.

name: Saphir
beer_styles: Pilsner, German Lager, Belgian Ale, Wheat
characteristics: Saphir hops have aroma descriptors that include spicy, fruity and floral with hints of tangerine tones.
heritage: Saphir hops were bred at the Hop Research Center in Hull. It was released to the public in 2002.

name: Sasquatch
characteristics: Sasquatch is Canada’s first patented, proprietary hop, grown only in Canada. Sasquatch is a unique dual-purpose hop with orange, tea, lemon citrus notes with an alpha content between 6.6 to 9%. One of the main reasons it was called Sasquatch is due to the rugged mountain terrain that it was found in as well as the massive hop cones that Sasquatch produces.
heritage: Based on a wild Canadian variety first found in Pemberton, British Columbia. It is Canada's first homegrown hop plant. Released to the public in 2018.

name: Satus
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, Stout, Barleywine, Imperial Stout
characteristics: Satus is a high alpha dual-use hop considered great as a clean foundational hop when used at the beginning of a boil and when an extra punch of hops is desired. When it is used late in the boil, strong citrus notes come to the forefront.
heritage: It was created at Yakima Chief Ranches, but appears to have been discontinued as of 2016.

name: Saugatuck
characteristics: Not much is written about the Saugatuck hop from GLH, but their description mentions that it is "fragrant", and hard to pin down but pleasant and interesting.
heritage: Saugatuck is a "field grade" hop created by Great Lake Hops (GLH).

name: Sequoia Blend
characteristics: Pronounced tropical fruit (mango, pineapple) with citrus and pine.
heritage: HS-Sequoia is a hop blend from the BSG Hop Solutions program, formerly known as HS-1228, available exclusively from Brewers Supply Group.

name: Shaddock
characteristics: Shaddock hops are described by GLH has having a bright & interesting lime and grapefruit aroma that also oddly has notes of onion and garlic.
heritage: Shaddock is a "field grade" hop created by Great Lake Hops.

name: Simcoe
beer_styles: Pale Ale, IPA, Double IPA, Lager, Wild Ale, Red Ale
hop_sub: Summit, Magnum (US), Amarillo, Mosaic, Cascade, Centennial, Citra, Columbus
characteristics:
Alongside its fruity and slightly earthy aromas, specific descriptors of the Simcoe hop includes grapefruit, passion fruit, pine and berry characteristics. In addition to its great bittering qualities, the Simcoe hop variety - often referred to as "Cascade on steroids" - is known for its tropical fruit flavors. It is commonly used in India pale ales and pale ales.

Simcoe is frequently used for bittering due to its high alpha acid percentages, but can be used as a late addition as well to bring out more fruity aromas. It has since become one of the most popular hops for both the craft beer and homebrewing industries. At 12%-14% Alpha Acid, Simcoe adds a smooth bitterness, but also packs a punch of stone fruit, pine, and citrus zest aromas. It truly is a dual purpose hop that is capable of standing on its own in single-hopped beers in a wide range of styles.
heritage:
Simcoe  - previously named YCR 14 - was created by Charles E. Zimmermann, developed by Select Botanicals Group, and released through Yakima Chief Ranches in 2000.

A patent was applied for the year prior to Simcoe’s release, making it a trademarked variety. Because of this proprietary status its parentage is a secret, but word is that it was derived via open pollination.
readmore: The Most Common Hops Used in IPAs|https://beermaverick.com/the-most-common-hops-used-in-ipas/
beer_examples: Temescal Simcoe IPA|https://www.temescalbrewing.com/beer-for-delivery/simcoe-single-hop-ipa;Hill Farmstead Simcoe Single Hop Pale Ale|https://hillfarmstead.com/beer/simcoe-single-hop-pale-ale/;Other Half DDH Simcoe Chroma|https://otherhalfbrewing.com/beer/ddh-simcoe-chroma/
statistics: In 2022, Simcoe moved up to #4 on the list of most grown US hops.|In 2019, Simcoe was the 5th most grown hop in the United States.|Between 2014-2019, Simcoe's harvest grew by 44%, the 6th highest increase in the United States.

name: Sitiva Blend
characteristics: Sitiva has ripe stone fruit, sweet floral, and soft peach flavors and aromas, as well as tropical fruit and lime character.
heritage: Added to the BSG Hop Solutions portfolio in 2020, Sitiva was developed through multiple rounds of brewing trials at the Rahr Technical Center pilot brewery. It is a blend formulated specifically for use in hazy IPA and other hop-forward beers.

name: Sládek
hop_sub: Saaz (CZ), Saaz (US), Northern Brewer (GR), Northern Brewer (US)
characteristics: Sládek hops have a fruity flavor profile with essences of peach, passion fruit, and grapefruit. Sládek is noted to be a good complement to Saaz in late-hopping applications.
heritage: Sládek was bred with breeding material that had an origin with Northern Brewer and Saaz.

name: Smaragd
beer_styles: Pilsner, German Ale, German Lager, Belgian Ale, Weissbier, Kolsch
characteristics: Smaragd has aroma descriptors that include floral, spicy and mild fruity. Smaragd is a fine aroma variety with many noble characteristics.
heritage: Smaragd is a new variety bred at the  Hop Research Institute in Hüll, Germany. Formerly known as Emerald.

name: Smooth Cone
characteristics: Smooth Cone is a New Zealand hop variety that is no longer grown commercially but can still be found around.
heritage: Smooth Cone is the offspring of an open pollination of a California Cluster and a sibling to First Choice in the 1960s.

name: Solero
beer_styles: India Pale Lager, Pale Ale, APA, NEIPA, IPA
characteristics: The Solero hop is a dual-purpose hop known to have strong fruit aromas and flavors including tropical and passion fruits along with mango.
heritage: Solero is a mix between Cascade and an unknown Hopsteiner male.

name: Sonnet
characteristics: Sonnet has a beautiful honeysuckle floral note with a sweet lemon citrus undertone.  Use it in classic English styles, and of course cask conditioned ales.
heritage: Sonnet Golding is Virgil Gamache Farms origination of the Saaz and English Kent Golding hops.

name: Sorachi Ace
beer_styles: Belgian Wits, IPA, Pale Ale, Belgian Ale, Saison
characteristics: Sorachi Ace hops have aroma descriptors that include lemon, lime and dill. It remains a  popular variety among craft brewers for its unique citrus fruit and herbal aromas.
heritage: Sorachi Ace is a cross between Brewer’s Gold, Saaz and Beikei No. 2 male. Developed in Japan in 1984 for Sapporo Breweries, Ltd.

name: Southern Aroma
characteristics: The aroma profile of the Southern Aroma hop includes floral, fields of flowers, woody/spicy, and fruity. It is a hop with classic noble hop character and a fruity South African terroir backdrop.
heritage: Southern Aroma's pedigree is a diploid seedling originating from Saaz and Hallertauer Mettelfreuh crossing.

name: Southern Brewer
characteristics: It is considered a bitter hop with no outstanding aroma qualities.
heritage: The first successful hop developed from the African Breweries Hops Farms Ltd. in 1972. An open pollinated cross of Fuggle.

name: Southern Cross
characteristics: Southern Cross hops have aroma descriptors that include citrus and tropical fruit with hints of lemon zest, lime, pine and spice. It has excellent essential oils and low co-humulone delivering a delicate balance of citrus and spice when added at the end of boil.
heritage: Southern Cross is a cross between NZ Smooth Cone and the result of a Californian and English Fuggle. It was released in 1994.

name: Southern Dawn
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, Lagers, Wheat Beers, Fruit Beers
characteristics: Southern Dawn is a great neutral bittering addition for hoppy styles like IPAs or Pale Ales.
heritage: Southern Dawn is a Southern Brewer crossed with OJA1/112.

name: Southern Passion
beer_styles: IPA, Session IPA, Pale Ales, Saison, Belgian Ale
characteristics: The aroma profile of the Southern Passion hop includes passion fruit, guava, red berries, floral, coconut, melon, calendula, and grapefruit.
heritage: Southern Passion is a South African bred aroma hop whose pedigree is a diploid seedling originating from Czech Saaz and German Hallertauer crossing.

name: Southern Promise
beer_styles: Lager, Pale Ale, Pilsner, Hefeweizen, Wheat Beers, IPA
characteristics: Southern Promise is a dual-purpose hop with smooth, clean bitterness and a woody, earthy fragrance.
heritage: Southern Promise diploid seedling from Southern Brewer and wild Slovenian male hop. It was released in 1992.

name: Southern Star
beer_styles: Amber Ale,Pale Ale,Cream Ale,Brown Ale,German Ales,IPA,Pale Lager,Fruit Beer,Stout, Porter
hop_sub: Mosaic, Ekuanot, El Dorado, Mandarina Bavaria, Southern Cross, Warrior
characteristics: This hop offers clean, crisp and efficient bittering with slight berry, floral and citrus notes. Detailed aroma descriptors include pineapple, blueberries, tangerine, tropical fruit, passion fruit, quince, pear, cassis, rose petals, orange and coffee.
heritage: Southern Star is a diploid seedling selected from a cross of the mother Outeniqua and father OF2/93.

name: Southern Sublime
characteristics: The aroma profile of Southern Sublime (N1/69) is Kool-Aid, Juicy Fruit, citrus, mango, plums, dank, and pungent. Works well with intensely hop-forward beers like modern IPAs. It also has classic noble hop character with a fruity terroir backdrop.
heritage: This is listed as a blend in some places, but BeerMaverick has not been able to confirm this.

name: Southern Tropic
characteristics: Southern Tropic is very fruit forward with sweet fruits of mango, plums, and grapefruit. Some brewers have described an aroma similar to Kool-Aid or Juicy Fruit. This hop works well with all hop-forward beers where tropical fruit characteristics are desired.
heritage: This is a blend of 80% Southern Aroma and 20% Southern Sublime.

name: Sovereign
characteristics: Sovereign hops have aroma descriptors that include pleasant but intense fruity flavors with mild floral, grassy, herbal, pear and mint characteristics. It is often used in conjunction with Goldings in English-style beers.
heritage: Sovereign is a dwarf variety developed in 1995 at Wye College by Peter Darby with open pollination. It was released in 2004. It is a granddaughter of Pioneer.

name: Spalt
hop_sub: Saaz (CZ), Tettnang, Ultra, Tettnanger, Santiam, Liberty, Hallertau
characteristics: Spalt - or Spalter - hops have aroma descriptors that include noble characteristics, earthy, spicy herbal and floral.
heritage: One of the world oldest hop varieties, Spalt hops date back as far as the 8th century.

name: Spalter Select
beer_styles: Kolsch, Belgian Ale, French Ale, Lager
hop_sub: Tettnanger, Saaz (CZ), Spalt, Perle (GR), Hallertau Tradition
characteristics: Sometimes referred to as just "Select", Spalter Select hops have aroma descriptors that include spicy and grass.
heritage: Spalter Select is a cross from 76/18/80 and 71/16/7, released in 1993 by the Hop Research Center in Hüll, Germany.  It was bred to be like the Spalt, Tettnang, and Saaz groups.

name: Sterling
characteristics: Sterling hops have aroma descriptors that include noble and spicy.
heritage: Sterling's pedigree includes Saaz, Cascade, Brewers Gold, Early Green, and other European varieties. It was bred in 1990 and released in 1998 by the USDA.

name: Sticklebract
beer_styles: Pale Ale, ESB, Stout, Porter, Lager, Pilsner, IPA
hop_sub: Northern Brewer (GR), Hersbrucker, Hallertau, Sorachi Ace, Simcoe
characteristics: Sticklebract was originally released as a high alpha bittering hop, but has become a dual-purpose variety characterized by citrus and pine flavors.
heritage: Sticklebract is a triploid variety and a result of an open pollinated NZ First Choice. It was developed by New Zealand Horticultural Research Center at Riwaka and released in 1972.

name: Sticky Fingers Blend
characteristics: A blend made up of 21 purely Michigan-grown hops with huge complex aroma notes of dankness, fruitiness and spice.
heritage: A blend made up of 21 purely Michigan-grown hops including Bitter Gold, Brewers Gold, Cascade, Cashmere, Centennial, Chinook, Columbus, Comet, Copper, Crystal, Fuggle, Galena, Mackinac, Magnum, Mittelfrueh, Nugget, Saaz, Summit, Tettnang, Triple Pearl and Wild Wolf.

name: Stirling
characteristics: The spicy characteristics of the Saaz play well with the bright citrus from the Cascade. While it is primarily used as an aroma hop, it is versatile enough to work in a wide range of styles and uses.
heritage: Sterling hop's parentage is readily apparent in this dual-purpose hop variety. Sterling is the daughter of Saaz and Cascade with some open pollination of German varieties.

name: Strata
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, Stout, Sours, Saison, Pale Ale, Lager
characteristics: The Strata hop has many layers of different fruit flavor, dried and fresh, anchored with a dried chili-cannabis-funk. Late hot side additions using Strata bring out layers of rounded-tropical plus bright-fresh fruit flavors; dry hopping yields more grapefruit and cannabis. Other descriptors include passion fruit, strawberry and dank.
heritage: The Strata hop is the result of an open pollinated Perle in Corvallis, Oregon in 2009. While it was born in 2009, it wasn't officially released until 2018.  Strata was Indie Hops’ first hop variety to be released out of their breeding program in cooperation with Oregon State University.

name: Strisselspalt
beer_styles: Amber Ale, Blonde Ale, Bock, Golden Ale, Pilsner, Saison
characteristics: Strisselspalt has a delicate, pleasant and well-balanced aroma. Due to its delicate aromas, Strisselspalt is ideal for use in late hop additions or in dry hopping. Some commonly used aroma descriptors for the Strisselspalt hop include spicy, citrusy, floral, fruity and herbal.
heritage:
Strisselspalt is the hop variety that is traditionally produced in Alsace, France. It is one of the classical fine aromatic varieties. 

It is also sometimes referred to as Strisselspalter.

name: Styrian Cardinal
characteristics: Cardinal hops are known to have a typical citrus and fruity notes as well as a harmonious flavor.
heritage:
Cardinal was developed and released by the Slovenian Institute of Hop Research and Brewing. Cardinal was bred from European and American germplasm. 

Sometimes referred to as just Cardinal.

name: Styrian Dragon
characteristics: Profile descriptions for the Styrian Dragon include floral citrus, grapefruit, lemon, berries, rose, and tropical fruit.  Works well with all hop-forward beers including American, Belgian, and British styles, especially modern IPAs.
heritage: Styrian Dragon was bred at the Slovenian Institute of Hop Research and Brewing in Žalec from European and American germplasm.

name: Styrian Eagle
characteristics: Styrian Eagle is a Double Purpose hop. An all-around good hop with a high alpha, good disease resistances and fruity and floral aroma. Characteristics include Floral, Peach, Pine
heritage: Slovenian origin. Developed and released by the Slovenian Institute of Hop Research and Brewing, it is a cross between a European and American variety.

name: Styrian Fox
characteristics: Styrian Fox is one of the latest Styrian hops on the market. When brewed, expect floral and fruity notes. Some brewers even say they get hints of pepper and mild tobacco flavours from this hop unique strain.
heritage: Developed by the Slovenian Institute for Hop Research and Brewing. It is a cross of a European and American variety.

name: Styrian Golding
beer_styles: English Ale, Oktoberfest, Belgian Ale, Pilsner, ESB, Barley Wine, Lager
characteristics: Styrian Golding hops have a spice with an earthy/sweet edge. It is a prized aroma hop and exhibits resinous, earthy flavors that are considered slightly more refined than Fuggle.
heritage:
Actually not a Golding variety at all, it is the result of a cloned of UK Fuggle. 

It is sometimes referred to as Savinjski Golding or Celeia.

name: Styrian Kolibri
characteristics: When Styrian Kolibri was used by craft brewers, they have noticed very attractive floral notes in a range of beer varieties.
heritage: Developed and released by the Slovenian Institute of Hop Research and Brewing, from European and American germplasm.

name: Styrian Wolf
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, British Ale, Belgian Ale
characteristics: Styrian Wolf hops have intense fruity and floral notes including flavors of sweet tropical fruits and complex aromas of mango, coconut, lemongrass, elderflower and even a touch of violet.
heritage: Styrian Wolf was developed by the Slovenian Institute for Hop Research and Brewing in Žalec Slovenia.

name: Sultana (Denali)
characteristics: Sultana was formerly called Denali, however it is also called Nuggetzilla by some. This hop is rich in pineapple, citrus (often perceived as lemon), and pine flavors, though it can often come off as spicy as well.
heritage: Sultana is a cross between Nugget, Zeus, and a USDA 19058 male. Its lineage is: 50% Nugget, 25% Zeus, and 25% 19058. Sultana was released as Denali in 2016, then renamed in 2019.
readmore: Why the Equinox, Denali and Stella Hops Changed Their Names|https://beermaverick.com/why-equinox-denali-and-stella-hops-changed-their-names/

name: Summer
characteristics: Summer hops have delicate notes of grass, stone fruit (peach), passion fruit, and citrus. It showcases distinct apricot and melon characteristics in dry hopping applications.  It is not as spicy as its Czech parent.
heritage:
Summer is the result of open pollination of a tetraploid Czech Saaz. It was bred in 1997 at Tasmanian Bushy Park Breeding Garden. 

2017 was the last harvest of Summer by HPA, as it was retired.

name: Summit
beer_styles: IPA, Pale Ale, Imperial IPA, Barleywine, Stout
hop_sub: Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Warrior, Millennium, Simcoe, Amarillo, Cascade
characteristics: Summit hops' aroma descriptors include pepper, incense, anise, orange, pink grapefruit and tangerine.  Summit is mainly used as a bittering hop, but does have earthy aromatic characteristics and subtle hints of citrus. Summit hops at times picks up notes of sulfur (like garlic/onion), so be careful when using this hop.
heritage: Summit is a semi-dwarf super high alpha hop variety released by the American Dwarf Hop Association in 2003. It is a cross between Lexus and an unspecified male derived from numerous hops including Zeus, Nugget and male USDA varieties.
statistics: In 2019, Summit was the 19th most produced hop in the United States.

name: Sun
characteristics: Sun hops have herbaceous aroma and flavor.
heritage: Thought to be derived from Brewers Gold and sister to Zeus by Hopsteiner. Appears to have been discontinued.

name: Sunbeam
characteristics: Sunbeam is an ornamental hop that was discovered to be inadvertently useful as an aroma hop. Selected in 1992 in Corvallis, Oregon.
heritage: Cultivated from a Saaz parent.

name: Super Galena
characteristics: Super Galena hops have aroma descriptors that include sweet fruits, pear, pineapple, blackcurrant, grapefruit, lime, gooseberry and spicy wood. Super Galena is comparable to Galena in its aroma and bitterness profile, but offers a substantially higher yield and complete resistance to all current hop powdery mildew strains found in the U.S.
heritage: Super Galena is a cross between 9801 and a USDA 19058m. It was released in 2006.

name: Super Pride
beer_styles: Bock, IPA, Lager, Pale Ale, Imperial Pale Ale
characteristics: Super Pride hops have aroma descriptors that include mild and pleasant subtle resin and fruit tones.
heritage: Super Pride is the daughter of Pride of Ringwood.

name: Sussex
beer_styles: English Ale, Pale Ale, Belgian Ale
characteristics: Sussex hops have aroma descriptors that include earthy, grass, mint, citrus and vanilla.
heritage: Sussex is a dwarf variety discovered in Northiam, East Sussex in 2005.

name: Sybilla
hop_sub: Perle (US), Perle (GR), Northern Brewer (US), Northern Brewer (GR), Magnum (GR), Magnum (US), Nugget, Bobek, Lubelski
characteristics: Sybilla hops include floral, citrus, chocolate, orange, gingerbread and tobacco aromas.
heritage: Sybilla is from a Lublin mother and wild Yugoslavian father. Released in 1996.

name: Sylva
beer_styles: Pilsner, Lager, California Common, Pale Ale
hop_sub: Hallertau Mittelfruh, Helga, Saaz (US), Northern Brewer (US)
characteristics: Sylva hops have flavors of the forest and fresh-cut timber, subtle and hoppy bohemian-style aroma.
heritage: The Sylva hop is a child of Saaz and was released in 1997. Also known as Southern Saaz.

name: Tahoma
characteristics: Tahoma hops have subtle flavors and aromas of the Tahoma hop include lemon citrus, orange, wood and spice.
heritage: Tahoma was released in 2013 by the USDA and Washington State University as a daughter of Glacier.

name: Taiheke
beer_styles: Ales, Pale Ales, IPA, New England IPA
characteristics: Remarkable levels of citrus notes that lean towards tropical fruit characteristics, such as grapefruit, lemon and lime. It is characterized by a floral, citrus profile, with a hint of spice.
heritage: First released in 1972, Taiheke is bred from crossing an English Fuggle and a male variety thought to be a crossing of Fuggle with the Russian variety of Serebrianka.

name: Talisman
characteristics: Talisman has been discontinued.
heritage: Talisman is descended from an open pollinated seedling of Late Cluster, selected in 1959.

name: Talus
beer_styles: Wheat Ale, Golden Ale, American style lagers, Pale Ales, India Pale Lager, India Pale Ale, Session IPA, New England IPA, Hazy IPA, Imperial IPA
characteristics: Previously called HBC 692, the newly named Talus is an aroma hop for whirlpool and dry hopping additions. It delivers a high intensity hop aroma well suited for IPAs and other hop-forward beers. Talus hops exhibit grapefruit, floral, stone fruit, potpourri, woody, cream, pine, and resinous notes.
heritage: Talus is the daughter of Sabro (HBC 438) and was open pollinated.

name: Tango
characteristics: Depending on the beer type and dosage, this hop can impart a strong citrus flavor with pineapple, passion fruit and cassis notes.
heritage: Tango is a cross between Cascade and a male Hüll breeding line.

name: Tardif de Bourgogne
characteristics: Having top notes of pine, frankincense and rose petals. It is a great alternative for those whose palates are dank and bleary, from the current onslaught of the sweet and heavy.  Tardif de Bourgogne is an old school French noble varietal that was commercially successful in lager beers prior to the introduction of the popular French Strisselspalt.  It’s name loosely translates to “late harvest of burgundy.”
heritage: Tardif de Bourgogne is a noble French varietal.

name: Target
beer_styles: British Ale, British Lager, Pale Ale
characteristics: Specific aroma descriptors of the Target hop include fresh green sage, spicy and peppery, and hints of citrus marmalade. Whirlpool or dry hop additions play up the spice character. Target offers crisp bittering and moderate alpha acids.
heritage: Target was released by Wye College in 1992.  It is a cousin of Challenger and descendant of Northern Brewer and Eastwell Goldings.

name: Teamaker
beer_styles: Teas, Cream Ale, Lager, Pale Ale
characteristics: Teamaker has the lowest alpha acid (it is not bitter at all) content of any commercially available variety, while its beta acids are higher than even Galena. This high beta-to-alpha ratio means, while Teamaker is not bitter, it is highly aromatic and still has all the antibacterial and health benefits of other hop varieties.
heritage: Teamaker's pedigree includes 31.25% Fuggle, 12.5% Late Grape, and 12.25% Late Cluster. The other 43.75% is not known. Teamaker was released by the USDA-ARS Forage, Seed, and Cereal Research Unit (FSCRU) for commercial use in 2006.

name: Tettnang (US)
hop_sub: Tettnanger, Fuggle, Santiam, Spalter Select, Spalt
characteristics: The American Tettnanger has noble characteristics.
heritage: Tettnanger is an cultivar of Fuggle most likely. Recent tests have shown it to be genetically distinct from the original land race, Tettnang Tettnanger.

name: Tettnanger
beer_styles: Wheat Beer, Bavarian Hefeweizen, Bitter, California Blonde Ale, Red Ale, Pilsner, Lager, American Amber Ale, Winter Ale, Pale Ale, Cream Ale, American
hop_sub: Tettnang (US), Spalter Select, Spalt, Saaz (CZ), Lubelski
characteristics: Tettnanger has notably more farnesene oil content giving it a soft spiciness and a subtle, balanced, floral and herbal aroma. It is great as a dual-use hop, and considered by many as being particularly well suited to European lagers and pilsners. Often used in Hefeweizen beers.
heritage: Tettnanger is a landrace variety bred in Germany. It is genetically similar to Saaz.
readmore: The Complete History of the Hefeweizen Yeast|https://beermaverick.com/the-complete-history-of-the-hefeweizen-yeast/

name: Tillicum
characteristics: Tillicum is a high alpha variety with a very high content of beta acids. The variety was developed through the John I. Haas, Inc. breeding program and released in 1995. It is a daughter of Galena and a full sister to Chelan and therefore has analytical data similar to both varieties.
heritage: Tillicum is the daughter of Galena (USDA 21182) x and Chelan USDA 21055 – USDA 63015M. The seedling selection was from a cross made in 1986, and it was selected for production in 1988. Released in 1995.

name: TNT Blend
characteristics: T'N'T blend is a fruity explosion of flavor on your tongue and unforgettably refreshing. It is a select blend of dynamite German hop varieties. This blend is designed specifically to major on the red fruits, sweet fruits and citrus to give a beer a solid tropical foundation.

name: Tomahawk
beer_styles: IPA, American Pale Ale, Stout, Barleywine, Lager
hop_sub: Columbus, CTZ, Zeus, Centennial, Chinook, Galena, Nugget, Millennium
characteristics: Tomahawk has aroma descriptors that include black pepper, licorice, curry and subtle citrus.
heritage: Tomahawk is a bittering hop of recent origin.  Tomahawk is often referred to as CTZ, a trio of similar hops including Columbus and Zeus. The exact lineage of Tomahawk is unknown, however it is widely assumed that Brewer’s Gold and several undisclosed American varieties played significant parenting roles. It was developed in the 1980s by Charles Zimmerman who had worked for the U.S. Department of Agriculture until 1979 and who subsequently held positions with various private hop-processing and trading companies.
readmore: The Rise and Eventual Fall of the CTZ Hop|https://beermaverick.com/the-rise-and-eventual-fall-of-the-ctz-hop/

name: Topaz
beer_styles: IPA, American Pale Ale, Bitter, Amber Ale
hop_sub: Galaxy, Citra, Cascade, Riwaka, Rakau, Amarillo
characteristics: Topaz hops have light, tropical fruit flavors of lychee, clove-like spice and resinous grassy tones. This is a fantastic hop to use when dry hopping. When used with later additions and in higher gravity brews, specific flavor qualities come out as pleasant, and more pronounced light tropical fruit.
heritage: Topaz was bred from an existing Australian high alpha acid variety crossed with pollen from a Wye college male. Topaz was created by the HPA breeding program in 1985 and commercialized in 1997. It did not find popularity until 2011.
statistics: Topaz is Australia's sixth most produced hop as of 2019.|In 2019, Australia produced 65 metric tonnes of Ella, which was a decrease of 5.8% over 2018.

name: Toyomidori
characteristics: Toyomidori has mild fruity flavors and a relatively high alpha percentage, so it is perfect for bittering.
heritage: Toyomidori is a cross between Northern Brewer (USDA 64107) and an open pollinated Wye male (USDA 64103M) and is also the parent of Azacca. it was produced in Japan for Kirin Brewery Co in 1981 and released in 1990. Also known as Kirin Flower and Feng Lv.

name: Trident
beer_styles: Hazy IPA, American Pale Ale, IPA, Lager
characteristics: Trident is a specialized blend with a much wider range of aroma and flavor characteristics than any single hop variety could generate on its own. Although, Trident is suitably balanced as is, it could also be added with others to build an even more unique and potent flavor profile.
heritage: Trident was released in 2019 with three unique Pacific Northwest-grown hop varieties.

name: TriplePearl
characteristics: Triple Pearl present complex aromas of black pepper, cedar/sandalwood, and floral perfume.
heritage: TriplePearl is a triploid daughter of Perle that was released by USDA-ARS in late 2013.

name: Triskel
beer_styles: Belgian Ale, Saison, Kolsch, Pilsner, Pale Ale, IPA, Lager, Wheat
characteristics: Triskel has inherited the aromatic notes of Strisselspalt in a particularly pronounced manner. Its notes are fruity, citrusy and flowery. Its oil content makes for interesting potential in late hopping, and especially in dry hopping. At the same time, its alpha acid content also creates an interesting alternative for first wort hopping.
heritage: Triskel is a cross developed in 2006 between the French Strisselspalt variety and the male plant of the English Yeoman variety.

name: Triumph
characteristics: Triumph hops have intense and fruity aromas with prominent peach, lime, and orange, followed with suggestions of spice and pine. Triumph is more delicate than other fruit forward varieties, but still robust enough to be used in a wide range of styles.
heritage: Triumph's parentage includes Nugget, Brewers Gold, East Kent Goldings, and Hallertau Mittelfruh. It was released in 2019.
readmore: Five Hop Varieties That Don’t Get Enough Love|https://beermaverick.com/five-hop-varieties-that-dont-get-enough-love/

name: Tropica
characteristics: Mighty Axe’s custom growing practices create Tropica’s distinct tropical character.
heritage: Grown from a Chinook by Mighty Axe Hops.

name: Tsingdao Flower
characteristics: Tsingdao Flower is the dominant hop grown in Gansu, China.  Also referred to as "Qingdao" Flower hops. Has a birght and crisp lemon-peel quality to the bitterness.
heritage: Bred from a Chinese version of Cluster.

name: Ultra
beer_styles: Oktoberfest, Blonde Ale, Wheat, Lager, Pilsner, Pale Ale, Bock
hop_sub: Hallertau, Crystal, Liberty, Mount Hood, Saaz (US), Tettnanger
characteristics: Ultra hops have aroma descriptors that include mild, spicy and floral bouquet. Ultra is related to traditional German varieties and can be utilized in similar applications.
heritage: Ultra hops are a combination of four parts Hallertau Mittelfrüh one part Saaz and one part an as unnamed varietal. This triplod is a half sister to Mt. Hood, Liberty and Crystal.

name: USDA 008
characteristics: USDA 008 is very fruit-forward with tangerine, meyer lemon, and stonefruit. There is a nice backdrop of fresh pine that makes it complex in a classic west coast IPA kind of way. Notes of green apple, peach, and mango are rounded out with grassy and subdued earth characteristics. A great choice for adding a new dynamic to West Coast IPAs and hoppy/hazy pale ales.
heritage: Nugget is 008's mother and was crossed with another experimental male. 008 was selected from the Elite growing line during Hopsource that was hosted by the Brewers Association here at Yakima Valley Hops.

name: Vanguard
beer_styles: Lager, Pilsner, Bock, Kolsch, Wheat, Munich Helles, Belgian Ale
characteristics: Vanguard hops have aroma descriptors that include woody and cedar notes. It is an aroma variety with similar characteristics to Hallertau Mittelfrüh. Vanguard is typically utilized in traditional German style beers as a noble type  variety.
heritage: Vanguard is a cross between a selected, Hallertauer daughter and a USDA selected, German aroma male; a triploid hop, similar to Hallertauer Mittelfrüh. It was bred in 1982 by the USDA and released in 1997.

name: Vic Secret
characteristics: Vic Secret is an Australian hop known for its bright tropical character of pineapple, pine, and passion fruit. Other aroma descriptors include tropical fruit, pine and herbs. It displays a more earthy character when added late in the boil, but is now commonly added as a whirlpool or dry hop in IPAs. Its flavors are similar to Galaxy hops, but are lighter in nature.
heritage: Vic Secret was created by the HPA breeding program in 2000 and commercialized in 2013. Its ancestry is a cross pollination of high alpha Australian and Wye College hops, which provides an interesting mix of English, European and North American heritage.
readmore: The Most Common Hops Used in IPAs|https://beermaverick.com/the-most-common-hops-used-in-ipas/
statistics: Vic Secret is Australia's second-most produced hop behind Galaxy, as of 2019.|In 2019, Australia produced 225 metric tonnes of Vic Secret, which was an increase of 10.8% over 2018.

name: Vienna Gold
characteristics: Vienna Gold is a hop variety that was grown widely in Australia several decades ago. However, due to the arrival of higher alpha varieties like Pride of Ringwood, it became less fashionable and was eventually discontinued. Vienna Gold is a highly vigorous growing variety that produces high yields of flowers and is easy to grow. This hop is not available commercially and is now only grown by homebrewers. It has a flavor similar to a German noble hop.
heritage: Some suggest the Vienna Gold hop is the same as Cluster.

name: Vista
characteristics: Brewers who participated in the blind smell tests described Vista as having Stone Fruit, Citrus, Tropical Fruit, and Floral characteristics. It can excel in fruit-forward IPAs and Pale Ales, but brewers also said it would work well in lighter, more delicate styles like lagers and pilsners.
heritage: They are the newest variety to be released by the USDA public breeding program. Vista is the result of a 2006 open pollinated cross by Dr. John Henning between the tetrapolid Perle female and an unknown diploid male.

name: Vital
characteristics: Vital hops have key flavors of plum, lavender, spice and licorice. It is very useful for bittering.
heritage: Vital was selected from breeding material with origin in Agnus. It was developed by the Zatac breeding program. it was registered in 2008.
statistics: Vital is the result of hop breeding for pharmaceutical and biomedical purposes and shows high contents of Xanthohumol and DMX.

name: Wai-iti
beer_styles: Pale Ale, IPA, Wheat Beer, Pilsner
characteristics: The New Zealand Wai-iti hop is lush and fragrant with dominant notes of stone fruit (notably peach and apricot) with pleasantly intense tropical citrus tones of lime and mandarin.
heritage: Wai-iti has a lineage consisting of notable varieties Hallertauer Mittelfruh as a 1/3 parent and Liberty as its grandparent. It was released to the public in 2011.
readmore: Southern Hemisphere Hops: Superlatives Edition|https://beermaverick.com/southern-hemisphere-hops-superlatives-edition/

name: Waimea
characteristics: Waimea hops have aroma descriptors that include lots of pine and citrus characters along with intense tangelo or mandarin.
heritage: Waimea as parentage stemming from Californian Late Cluster, Fuggle and Saaz. It was released in 2012.

name: Wakatu
beer_styles: Belgian Ale, Lager, Pale Ale, Pilsner
characteristics: Wakatu has a pronounced earthy-sweet floral Hallertau character underscored by tropical fruit notes. Other specific aroma descriptors include restrained floral notes and freshly zested lime.
heritage: Wakatu is a triplod variety that came from an open pollination of Hallertau Mittelfruh and a New Zealand-derived male. It was released in 1988, as Hallertau Aroma, Wakatu was renamed in 2011.

name: Walhalla
characteristics: GLH says that Walhalla hops have a light citrus aroma with a hint of garlic (very faint), grassiness and herbal notes.
heritage: Walhalla are "field grade" hops created by Great Lake Hops.

name: Warrior
hop_sub: Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Nugget, Magnum (GR), Magnum (US), Summit
characteristics: Warrior has mild aromas with notes of resin and subtle pine. It was created for its high alpha content, low co-humulone, good storage stability and tolerance to powdery mildew. It is primarily used for its mild, clean bittering properties.
heritage: Warrior hops were developed by Select Botanicals Group and Yakima Chief Ranches.

name: Whitbread Golding Variety (WGV)
beer_styles: Scottish Ale, Bitter, Pale Ale, Marzen, Amber Ale
characteristics: Whitbread Golding hops have fragrantly woodsy with overtones of fresh garden herbs and green fruit, thanks to good proportions of humulene and farnesene. It displays many similar characteristics but has more robust and slightly sweet, hoppy flavor.
heritage: Whitbreads Golding Variety likely has Fuggle parentage and was bred from Bates' Brewer in 1911. WGV is not a true Golding variety.

name: Wild Loyalist
characteristics: With an alpha and beta profile similar to the noble hops, our Wild Loyalist contributes mild black pepper, herbal, earth, with a floral backend.
heritage: Wild Loyalist has evolved into it's current incarnation growing in local hedgerows and abandoned fields since the United Empire Loyalist pioneers originally brought it to Prince Edward County.

name: Wild Wolf
characteristics: Wild Wolf has soft fruit, herbal and natural earthy notes that make this a nice, approachable hop.
heritage: Wild Wolf is a new hop variety found by Custer, Michigan.

name: Willamette
beer_styles: English Ale, American Pale Ale, Brown Ale, American Lager, Porter, ESB
hop_sub: Glacier, Fuggle, Tettnang (US), Styrian Golding, East Kent Golding
characteristics: Willamette hops have aroma descriptors that include floral, incense, and elderberry.
heritage: Willamette is a triploid seedling of the English Fuggle variety. It was released in 1971 immediately after USDA approval the same year.
statistics: In 2019, Willamette was the 13th most produced hop in the US.|Between 2014 and 2019, Willamette's production grew by 18%, which is the 23rd fastest hop in the US

name: Wurttemberg
characteristics: Wurttemberg is a legacy variety that carries a kaffir lime citrus and floral notes, as well as a pleasantly soft expression of pine and oregano spice.
heritage: Wurttemberg is a sister to Tettnanger and named after Baden-Wurttemberg, the third largest state of Germany.

name: X13459
characteristics: This aroma cultivar is new in 2020 and known to have strong fruit flavors including citrus, peach and strawberry.
heritage: A mix between Cascade and Wye Target.

name: XJA2/436
characteristics: This hop has some potent bittering qualities. The aroma profile of XJA/436 is bergamot citrus, dried lemon zest, candied papaya, ripe cantaloupe, gooseberries, and resin.

name: Yakima Cluster
characteristics: Traits of the Yakima Cluster hop include a moderate bittering, some earthy flavors and a flowery aroma with elements of sweet fruit. All Cluster hops are interchangeable in brewing and quality.
heritage: Yakima Cluster is the daughter of Late Cluster and the granddaughter of Pacific Coast Cluster and was first grown in the US in 1957. The Late Cluster variety is a sister selection of L8 (USDA 65104), and Yakima Cluster (USDA 65102).

name: Yakima Gold
characteristics: Yakima Gold hops are an excellent general purpose variety with smooth bitterness and pleasant aroma characteristics.
heritage: Released by Washington State University in 2013, Yakima Gold is a cross between Early Cluster and a native Slovenian male.

name: Yellow Sub
characteristics: This composition turns a fine and excellent hop aroma into a sweet, fruity flamenco of apricot and orange, with a hint of blackberries. It's been described as "Amarillo on steroids".

name: Yeoman
characteristics:
Yeoman has a strong English hop aroma with citrus notes.

Discontinued.
heritage: Yeoman is a cross made at Wye College, England in the 1970s. This hop is responsible for Pioneer, Super Pride and Pride of Ringwood.

name: YQH-1380
characteristics: This variety is described as "Clean and bright. With tropical, citrus, and stone fruit." Sensory analysis of the crop picks out aromas of pineapple, guava, lime, white peach, and orange zest.
heritage: From Yakima Quality Hops and was available for the first time in 2022. It was "found" at YQH.

name: Zagrava
characteristics: The Zagrava hop has spicy-floral aroma with herbal and fruit notes.

name: Zamba Blend
characteristics:
Complex aroma characterized by juicy tropical fruits (pineapple, mango), stone fruits, candy, and orange tangerine. This hop blend
is named for the overwhelming tropical dance explosion its aroma evokes.
heritage: Zamba is the first proprietary release in the BSG Hop Solutions Program, available exclusively from Brewers Supply Group.

name: Zappa
characteristics: Zappa hops have spicy aromas with intense notes of tropical fruit (mango, passion fruit), citrus, and pine. Additional descriptors include savory, mint, and Fruity Pebbles. This hop is unlike any other on the market today.
heritage: Zappa was recovered in the wild in the mountains of New Mexico. It is a 100% Humulus lupulus var. neomexicanus aroma hop found by CLS Farms.

name: Zenia
characteristics: Has intense dank and resin, jammy-orange aromas and distinctive lack of onion-garlic flavor. By manipulating the harvest timing and processing practices of CTZ hops, Zenia becomes the intersection between heavy-weight resin and orange marmalade.
heritage:
Zenia has CTZ genetics and is produced by Mighty Axe Hops. 

In 2020, Mighty Axe Hops shut their doors due to a combination of a large storm that demolished their crops and the COVID-19 crisis.

name: Zenith
characteristics: Zenith hops have pleasing bouquet aromas with hoppy citrus flavors.
heritage: Zenith is a seedling selection from a cross made at Wye College, England in the 1970s.

name: Zeus
beer_styles: IPA, American Pale Ale, Stout, Barleywine, Lager
hop_sub: Columbus, Tomahawk, Zeus, CTZ, Centennial, Chinook, Galena, Nugget, Millennium
characteristics: Zeus hops have aroma descriptors that include pungent, black pepper, licorice, and curry.
heritage: Zeus is a daughter of Nugget. Although genetically different, Zeus is often referred to as part of CTZ along with Columbus and Tomahawk, a trio of similar hops. The exact lineage of Zeus is unknown, however it is widely assumed that Brewer’s Gold and several undisclosed American varieties played significant parenting roles.
readmore: The Rise and Eventual Fall of the CTZ Hop|https://beermaverick.com/the-rise-and-eventual-fall-of-the-ctz-hop/

name: Zula
characteristics: Zula was designed as high-alpha variety, but due to “additional aroma compounds” it was sentenced to death by larger breweries. Years later it turned out that those strange aromas are tropical and citrus fruits. This hop is accompanied by short, clean bitterness when used in the early boil.
heritage: Zula has a pedigree of Lubelski, a Savinski Golding hybrid and a Yugoslavian male. It is a twin sister of Lunga and was released to the public in 2004.

name: Zythos
characteristics: Zythos hops impart distinct tangerine, citrus, floral, pine and grapefruit tones.
heritage: Zythos is a proprietary hop blend created by Hopunion.
